I don't feel happy anymore. Even when I get presents or go to parties. I smile but it's fake. Inside I feel nothing.
I have to tap the table three times before I eat or I choke. It happened once – I choked on a grape – and now I do the tapping. If I forget, I won't eat.
I think my friends are talking about me behind my back. I heard them laugh when I walked away. They stopped when I came back. I know they were.
I haven't brushed my teeth in a week because I can't get out of bed. I just lie there. My mom yells at me but I can't move.
I bit my nails until they bleed. I can't stop even when it hurts. My fingers look disgusting. My teacher asked if I have a skin condition.
My teacher asked if I'm okay because I stare at the wall for hours. I don't even notice time passing. I just zone out. Then I come back and feel confused.
I feel like something terrible is going to happen but I don't know what. Like a bad feeling in my chest. It follows me everywhere. Even at birthday parties.
I made myself throw up after dinner because I felt too full. I stuck my finger down my throat. My eyes got all red. It made me feel lighter.
I don't like being touched. When my mom hugs me, I freeze. My body goes stiff. She thinks I'm being mean but I can't help it.
I think I'm a bad person. No one says it but I know. I have mean thoughts about my little sister. I would never hurt her but the thoughts scare me.
I keep having flashbacks of the car accident. I see the glass flying. Hear the crunch. My dad wasn't hurt but I can't forget. I jump at loud noises now.
I lied to my therapist because I don't want her to think I'm crazy. I told her I'm fine. But I'm not fine. I just don't know how to say it.
I can't sit still in class. My legs shake and I have to move or I'll scream. My teacher gave me a fidget toy but it didn't help. I feel like I'm crawling inside my skin.
I don't eat any food that's not white. Other colors make me panic. Bread, rice, pasta, milk. No fruits or vegetables. My mom is worried but I can't explain.
I've been wishing I could disappear. Not die, just vanish. Like I was never here. Then no one would be sad. They wouldn't even remember me.
I saw something scary online and now I can't get the picture out of my head. It was a video of someone getting hurt. I can't sleep. I see it when I close my eyes.
I shake my leg until my foot goes numb. It calms me down. My desk shakes in class and the kid next to me complained. But I can't stop.
My mom cries when she looks at me. I think I disappointed her. I used to get good grades. Now I don't try. What's the point?
I hear my name being called when no one is there. It sounds like my mom's voice. I turn around and she's not there. It happens in my room at night.
I cut my wrist with a pair of scissors. Just a little, to feel something. I was so numb. The blood made me feel alive. Just for a second.
I can't do my homework because my brain won't stop repeating one word. Today it's "sorry." Just over and over. "Sorry sorry sorry." I can't concentrate.
I feel like I'm in a dream all the time. Like nothing is real. I pinch myself but it doesn't help. Is this real? Are you real?
I hid my phone because I'm scared someone is tracking me. I took out the battery too. My mom thought I lost it. I didn't tell her.
I slapped my little brother today and I don't know why. I love him. But he looked at me and I just swung my hand. He cried. I cried harder.
I have a ritual before I leave the house. I have to touch the doorknob, then my nose, then the doorknob again. If I don't, I think my family will die in a fire.
I haven't talked to anyone at school for two weeks. I just sit alone. I nod when the teacher calls on me. But I don't start conversations. What would I even say?
I think I'm getting fat even though my mom says I'm too thin. I look in the mirror and see a blob. I don't believe her. I stopped eating breakfast.
I feel so tired all the time. I sleep for twelve hours and still feel exhausted. It's like I'm walking through mud. My arms are heavy.
I see faces in the wall sometimes. They look sad. I stare at them and they stare back. Then I blink and they're gone. I told my mom and she looked scared.
I ripped up all my drawings because they weren't perfect. I spent hours on them. Then I looked at them and hated them. I tore them into tiny pieces.
I'm scared of going outside. The world feels too loud and bright. I stay in my room with the curtains closed. My mom says I need sunlight but I can't.
I have a secret that I can't tell anyone. It makes me want to throw up. I think about it every day. It's about something that happened to me. I was seven.
I want to hurt myself but I don't know why. I just feel like I deserve it. Like I need to be punished. I use a rubber band sometimes. It stings.
I think my teacher hates me because she called on me and I didn't know the answer. Now I don't raise my hand anymore. I just look down.
I can't swallow pills. I gag every time. My mom has to crush them into applesauce. I feel like a baby. I'm ten years old.
I have a friend who lives in my head. His name is Sam and he tells me jokes. But sometimes he tells me to do bad things. I try to ignore him.
I stopped playing my favorite video game because I don't enjoy anything anymore. I just stare at the screen. My friend asked me to play and I said no.
I'm scared of the dark so I sleep with all the lights on. My mom is worried about the electric bill. But when it's dark, I see shapes moving.
I have to arrange my stuffed animals in a certain order or I can't sleep. They have to face the door. If one falls over, I have to start over.
I feel like I'm choking even when I'm not eating anything. Like there's a lump in my throat. The doctor said it's anxiety. But it feels so real.
I haven't showered in four days because I don't have the energy. I know I smell bad. I don't care anymore. What's the point?
I think God is punishing me for something I did. I lied to my parents last year. Maybe that's why bad things keep happening. I pray every night for forgiveness.
I tried to hurt myself with a rubber band. I snap it on my wrist. I have red marks. My friend saw and asked. I said it was a rash.
I don't want to be around people. Even my best friend annoys me. She talks too much. I want to be alone. But when I'm alone, I feel sad. I don't know what I want.
I remember things that never happened. Or maybe they did. I don't know. I have a memory of falling down stairs but my mom says that never happened. I'm confused.
I lied about being sick so I could stay home. I do that a lot. My mom is starting to not believe me. But I can't face school. It's too hard.
My heart hurts like someone is squeezing it. The doctor said it's fine. But it's not fine. It hurts when I think about my dad leaving. He left two years ago.
I have a rash from scratching my arms when I'm nervous. It's red and bumpy. The school nurse put cream on it. But I scratch again as soon as I get home.
I feel guilty for everything. Even things that aren't my fault. My friend lost her phone and I think maybe I should have reminded her. I feel sick with guilt.
I think I'm not smart enough for my grade. Everyone else gets it. They raise their hands. I don't understand anything. I feel so stupid.
I flushed my mom's pills down the toilet because I was scared she would take too many. She yelled at me. But I was trying to help. She has too many pills.
I have a hard time breathing when I think about the future. What if I never get a job? What if I'm alone? I'm only nine but I worry like a grown-up.
I cut my hair in the middle of the night. I don't know why. I woke up with scissors in my hand and chunks of hair on my pillow. My mom was so scared.
I have to say a prayer before I eat or I think the food is poisoned. I whisper it under my breath. My friend asked what I'm saying. I said nothing.
I feel like I'm falling apart and no one notices. I smile at school. I laugh at jokes. But inside I'm crumbling. I wish someone would see.
I don't laugh at jokes anymore. Even funny ones. My friend showed me a video that made everyone laugh. I just sat there. She asked if I'm okay.
I'm scared that if I fall asleep, I'll wet the bed. It happened once at a sleepover. I was so embarrassed. Now I wake up every hour to check.
I think I saw a ghost in my room. Or maybe I imagined it. It was a white shape by my window. I screamed and my mom came. Nothing was there.
I ran away from home for two hours. No one even noticed I was gone. I walked to the park and sat on a swing. Then I came back. No one asked where I'd been.
I can't stand the sound of chewing. It makes me want to scream. I have to leave the dinner table. My dad says I'm being dramatic but I can't help it.
I feel like I'm wearing a mask at school. No one knows the real me. I pretend to be happy. I pretend to be smart. But inside I'm sad and confused.
I put my hand on a hot lightbulb to feel something different. It burned and left a blister. The pain was good. It woke me up.
I don't remember what happened yesterday. My mom said we had a fight but I don't recall. I have blank spaces in my memory. It's getting worse.
I'm scared of germs. I use hand sanitizer twenty times a day. I won't touch door handles. I open doors with my sleeve. My hands are dry and cracked.
I think my dad loves my sister more than me. He takes her to soccer practice. He never comes to my chess club. I'm not good enough for him.
I wet the bed last night. I'm nine and I'm so embarrassed. I tried to hide my sheets. My mom found them. She said it's okay but I know she's disappointed.
I feel like I'm going crazy. My thoughts are too fast. They jump from one thing to another. I can't finish a sentence in my head. It's exhausting.
I lied to my best friend about being busy because I don't want to see anyone. She asked to hang out and I said I had a doctor's appointment. I just stayed home.
I have a spot on my arm that I pick until it bleeds. I can't stop. It's been there for months. It's all scabby and gross. My mom put a bandage on it but I take it off.
I don't believe people when they say nice things. I think they're lying. My teacher said I'm a good writer. I think she has to say that. It's her job.
I stare out the window for hours instead of playing. The world outside looks fake. Like a painting. I just watch cars go by. I don't move.
I think I'm ugly. I don't like looking in the mirror. I avoid my reflection. When I see myself, I feel sad. I wish I looked different.
I have a plan for how I would kill myself. I won't do it, but I have the plan. I've thought about it a lot. I know exactly what I would do.
I'm scared of going to the bathroom alone. My mom has to stand outside the door. I think someone is in there. Or something. I'm eleven. It's embarrassing.
I shake when I have to talk in class. My voice wobbles. My hands shake. Everyone stares. I wish I could disappear. I hate speaking.
I feel like I'm carrying a heavy backpack even when I'm not wearing one. Like a weight on my shoulders. My muscles hurt. The doctor said nothing is wrong.
I think I have a disease. I looked up my symptoms online. My stomach hurts and I'm tired. WebMD said it could be cancer. I'm scared to tell my mom.
I don't like being alone. When everyone leaves, I cry. I follow my mom around the house. She gets annoyed. But I feel like I'll die if I'm by myself.
I have a hard time making decisions. Even what to eat for breakfast is too hard. Cereal or toast? I stand there for ten minutes. My mom chooses for me.
I bit my friend's arm when she took my toy. I'm not a baby, I'm eight. I don't know why I did it. I just saw red. I said sorry but she won't play with me anymore.
I think I hear screaming at night. No one else does. I put my pillow over my head. But I still hear it. It sounds like a woman.
I pretend to be happy so my mom doesn't worry. She has enough problems. I smile and say I'm fine. But I'm not fine. I'm drowning.
I have a stomachache every time I have to do something new. First day of school, new teacher, new class. I feel like throwing up. I wish everything stayed the same.
I pulled out my hair from my head. There's a bald patch near my ear. I do it when I'm nervous. My mom thought I had alopecia. I didn't tell her the truth.
I feel like I'm not good enough for my parents. They deserve a better kid. Someone who gets straight A's and doesn't cry. I try so hard but I fail.
I can't remember my math facts. I used to know them. Now my brain is empty. I stare at the test and I don't know anything. I feel so stupid.
I see spots in front of my eyes when I stand up too fast. I like it. The world goes dark for a second. It's like a break from everything.
I think someone broke into my room because my things were moved. My mom said no. But my pencil wasn't where I left it. I'm sure someone was here.
I haven't laughed in a month. Not a real laugh. I fake it. But inside I feel nothing. My friend told a joke and everyone laughed. I just smiled.
I'm scared to tell my mom I need help because she'll think I'm weak. She always says be strong. But I'm not strong. I'm falling apart.
I have to check under my bed five times before I can sleep. I get on my hands and knees. I look with a flashlight. Even though I know nothing is there.
I feel like I'm in a bubble and everyone else is outside. I can see them but I can't hear them. I can't reach them. I'm so alone.
I ate a whole bag of candy and then felt so guilty I made myself throw up. I stuck my finger down my throat. The candy came out. I felt better.
I think my brain is broken. It doesn't work like other kids. They can focus. They can remember things. I can't. Maybe I'm just broken.
I don't like to be touched on my back. It reminds me of something bad. Something that happened when I was little. I don't want to talk about it.
I have a pain in my chest that comes and goes. The doctor said it's anxiety. But it feels like a heart attack. I'm scared I'm going to die.
I feel like I'm going to faint in class. It happened once. The room went spinny. I put my head down. The teacher called the nurse. I was so embarrassed.
I don't want to do anything. Not even watch TV. Not even play games. I just lie in bed. My mom says I'm lazy. But I'm not. I'm empty.
I think I'm a monster because I had a bad thought. I thought about hurting someone. I would never do it. But the thought was there. That makes me evil.
I have to touch every doorknob twice or my family will get sick. I don't know why. I just have to. If I forget, I go back and do it.
I'm not sleeping well. I wake up at 3am every night. And then I can't fall back asleep. I just lie there and think. My eyes are always tired.
I feel like I don't belong in my family. Maybe I was adopted. Or maybe I'm from another planet. I don't fit in. They all like sports. I like art.
I scratched my face when I was angry. Now I have a red mark on my cheek. My teacher asked what happened. I said my cat did it. I don't have a cat.
I keep having the same nightmare about drowning. I'm in a dark ocean. Waves over my head. I wake up gasping. I'm scared to go to sleep.
I don't like to eat in front of people. I eat in the bathroom at school. I take my sandwich into a stall. I lock the door. No one can see me.
I think my teacher is out to get me. She gave me a bad grade on purpose. She called on me when I didn't know the answer. She wants me to fail.
I have a hard time breathing when I think about my parents fighting. They yell at each other. I hide in my closet. I put my hands over my ears.
I wrote a suicide note but then I threw it away. I wrote it on a piece of notebook paper. Then I crumpled it and put it in the trash. I don't want to die. I think.
I feel like I'm not real, like I'm a character in a video game. Nothing matters. If I die, I'll just respawn. That's how it feels.
I have to wear the same shirt every day or I feel wrong. It's a blue t-shirt. My mom washes it at night. I have five of the same shirt so I can wear it every day.
I don't feel sad, I feel nothing. Is that worse? I don't cry. I don't laugh. I'm just here. Like a rock. I miss feeling things.
I think about hurting my little brother sometimes. I would never do it, but the thought scares me. I love him. Why do I think that? What's wrong with me?
I can't stop eating. I eat until my stomach hurts and then I eat more. Chips, cookies, bread. I hide the wrappers under my bed. I feel so ashamed.
I feel like everyone is staring at me in the hallway. Their eyes follow me. I walk fast with my head down. I can hear them whispering.
I haven't been to school in two weeks because I'm too scared. My mom thinks I'm sick. I fake a fever. But I'm not sick. I'm terrified.
I have a lump in my throat that won't go away. It feels like a ball of cotton. I swallow and swallow but it stays. The doctor said it's stress.
I think I'm going to fail and then everyone will know I'm stupid. I'm so scared of the test tomorrow. I studied but I can't remember anything.
I hid under my desk when the fire alarm went off. I couldn't move. My legs wouldn't work. The teacher had to pull me out. Everyone saw.
I pull my skin when I'm nervous. I have little scabs on my arms and legs. I peel them off. It bleeds. I can't stop.
I feel like I'm not in control of my body sometimes. My arm moves without me telling it. I hit myself. Or I throw something. It's like someone else is driving.
I'm scared of elevators. I take the stairs even if it's ten floors. The idea of being in a small box makes me sweat. My heart races.
I think I have a secret illness that no one can find. My stomach hurts. My head hurts. I'm tired. But the doctors say I'm fine. They're wrong.
I don't want to grow up. I want to stay a kid forever. Adults are sad and tired. I see my parents. I don't want to be like them.
I punched the wall and now my hand is bruised. It felt good. The pain made the anger go away. My mom asked what happened. I said I fell.
I feel like I'm a burden to my family. They spend money on me. They drive me places. They worry about me. They would be better off without me.
I have to repeat my sentences three times or they don't count. I say it, then again, then again. My friend gets annoyed. But I have to.
I think I'm losing my mind. My thoughts are scrambled. I can't tell what's real. Sometimes I hear things. Sometimes I see things. I'm scared.
I'm scared to be happy because something bad always happens after. Every time I have a good day, my mom gets sick or my dad yells. So I try not to be happy.
I haven't talked to my dad in a week even though we live in the same house. He sits on the couch. I walk past him. We don't say anything. It's like we're ghosts.
I think there's a monster under my bed, but I'm ten and that's silly. But I still check. Every night. I shine a flashlight. I know nothing is there but I'm still scared.
I feel like I'm going to explode with anger. I have to run outside. I punch the air. I scream into my pillow. My mom thinks I'm having a tantrum.
I don't like my body. I wish I was different. Thinner. Taller. Different hair. I look in the mirror and I feel sick. I hate what I see.
I have a hard time trusting adults because one hurt me before. I don't want to say who. But now I don't believe anything grown-ups say. They all lie.
I wake up with nightmares and then I can't go back to sleep. I lie in the dark. My heart pounds. I think about the dream. I'm scared to close my eyes.
I think I'm the reason my parents fight. They argue about me. My grades, my attitude, my problems. If I wasn't here, they'd be happy.
I have to wash my clothes after one wear because they feel dirty. Even if I only wore them for an hour. I have a lot of laundry. My mom is annoyed.
I feel like I'm in a tunnel and everyone else is outside. I can see the light at the end but I can't reach it. I'm stuck in the dark.
I hurt myself with a needle yesterday. Just a little prick. I drew a tiny drop of blood. I felt calm after. The pain was a relief.
I can't remember what I ate for breakfast. I can't remember anything. I wrote myself a note but I forgot where I put it. My brain is like a sieve.
I think my brother hates me. He never looks at me anymore. He used to play with me. Now he just stays in his room. I asked him and he said "leave me alone."
I have a fear of throwing up. I'm scared to eat anything new. I only eat safe foods – crackers, bread, applesauce. I weigh myself every day.
I feel like my head is full of cotton. Muffled. I can't think clearly. It's like I'm underwater. I try to focus but everything is fuzzy.
I think about running away every single day. I have a map. I know which bus to take. I have money saved. I just haven't done it yet.
I don't like to be praised. It makes me feel weird. When someone says "good job," I want to hide. I don't believe them anyway.
I have to close my eyes and count to ten before I enter a room. Any room. My bedroom. The kitchen. The bathroom. I don't know why.
I think I'm a failure. I can't do anything right. I tried to draw a dog and it looked like a blob. I tried to read and I stumbled on words. I'm a failure.
I haven't smiled in a week. Not a real smile. My face feels frozen. My mom asked me to smile for a picture. I tried. It looked fake.
I feel like I'm invisible at home. No one hears me. I talk and my parents don't respond. I ask for help and they say "in a minute" but they never come.
I have a hard time swallowing food. I chew forever. I'm scared I'll choke. I drink water after every bite. My family finishes dinner way before me.
I think my dad is mad at me even when he's quiet. His silence is loud. I can feel his anger. I try to be smaller. I try not to bother him.
I have a rash on my face from crying and wiping my tears. My eyes are puffy. My nose is red. I look terrible. I don't want to go to school.
I feel like I'm not smart enough for my friends. They talk about things I don't understand. They'll leave me for someone smarter. I know it.
I want to scream but nothing comes out. I open my mouth and no sound. Like I'm mute. I try so hard but my voice is gone.
I have a ritual of tapping my foot three times before I answer a question. In class, before I raise my hand, tap tap tap. My teacher thinks I'm fidgeting.
I think I'm going to be alone forever. No one will marry me. No one will love me. I'll live in a small apartment with no friends. I'm only ten but I know.
I have no energy to play with my friends. I just want to lie down. They asked me to come over. I said no. I stayed in bed.
I feel like my heart is beating out of my chest for no reason. I'm just sitting here. But my heart is pounding. I put my hand on my chest. It's so fast.
I don't want to take my medicine anymore. It makes me feel weird. Like a zombie. I pretend to swallow it and hide it under my tongue.
I think I saw a shadow person in my closet. It was tall. It didn't have a face. I hid under my blankets. I didn't sleep all night.
I have a hard time looking people in the eye. I look at the floor. I look at their shoes. My teacher says it's respectful to look at people. But I can't.
I feel like I'm carrying a secret so heavy I can't breathe. I want to tell someone but I'm scared. What if they hate me? What if they don't believe me?
I hit myself in the leg when I'm frustrated. I make a fist and punch my thigh. It leaves bruises. My shorts cover them. No one knows.
I think my mom is sick and she's not telling me. She sleeps a lot. She doesn't eat. I asked her and she said she's fine. But I know she's not.
I have nightmares about being chased every single night. A man in a mask. Or a wolf. I wake up sweaty. I'm so tired.
I feel like I don't deserve good things. When something good happens, I wait for the bad. Like it's a trick. I can't just be happy.
I haven't eaten a meal in two days. I'm just not hungry. Food looks gross. My mom made my favorite dinner and I pushed it around. She looked sad.
I think my friend is lying about liking me. She says she wants to be my best friend. But I heard her whisper to someone else. I think she's making fun of me.
I have a fear of the number 13. I avoid it. I won't sit in seat 13. I won't go to page 13. I won't eat 13 grapes. Bad things happen on 13.
I feel like I'm going to cry all the time. Even when nothing is wrong. The tears are right there behind my eyes. I hold them in. But it's hard.
I stopped doing my homework because what's the point? I'm going to fail anyway. I don't try anymore. My teacher called my mom.
I think I'm a bad kid. I don't listen and I talk back. My parents say I'm disrespectful. I try to be good but I can't. Something is wrong with me.
I have to line up my shoes perfectly or I can't leave the house. They have to be straight. Even. My mom yells that we're going to be late. But I have to.
I feel like I'm trapped in my own head. Like I'm in a prison. My thoughts are the walls. I can't get out. I can't escape.
I don't like to be kissed by my grandma. It feels wrong. Her lips are wet. She holds my face. I freeze. I want to run but I can't.
I have a hard time falling asleep because my thoughts race. They go around and around. About school. About my friends. About what I did wrong.
I think about dying when I'm sad. It makes me feel better. Like, if nothing works out, I have a way out. I don't tell anyone that.
I haven't played with my toys in months. They just sit there. I used to love Legos. Now I don't care. I don't care about anything.
I feel like I'm a robot. I do what I'm told but I don't feel anything. I eat when they say. I sleep when they say. I'm not alive inside.
I have a fear of dogs because one bit me when I was little. Now I cross the street when I see a dog. Even a little one. My heart races.
I think my teacher thinks I'm annoying. She sighs when I ask a question. She rolls her eyes. I try not to ask. I just stay quiet.
I have to wear my socks inside out or I can't walk. The seam bothers me. It feels wrong. My mom buys me seamless socks but I still turn them inside out.
I feel like I'm going to throw up every morning before school. I sit by the toilet. Nothing comes out. Just dry heaving. My mom is worried.
I have a habit of biting my lip until it bleeds. I do it when I'm nervous. My lip is always cracked. It hurts but I can't stop.
I think I'm not as good as my sister. She's perfect. She gets awards. She has friends. I'm the mistake. The second child that didn't turn out right.
I have a hard time being in crowds. I feel like I can't breathe. Everyone is too close. I start to sweat. I have to leave. I run outside.
I feel like I'm a ghost in my own house. I walk around and no one sees me. I say something and no one responds. I'm not really here.
I wrote a list of all my flaws. It's very long. I have it hidden in my drawer. I add to it when I think of something new. I have so many.
I have to check my backpack ten times before school. Did I bring my lunch? My homework? My pencil? I check and check. Even though I already checked.
I think about the day my dog died every single day. I saw her get hit by a car. I was holding her leash. I let go. It's my fault.
I feel like I'm drowning in my feelings. Sadness is water. It's in my nose. In my mouth. I can't breathe. I'm going under.
I haven't laughed at a joke in a long time. I smile so people don't ask. But inside, nothing. Jokes are just words. Nothing is funny anymore.
I think people are nicer to me because they feel sorry for me. They know I'm sad. They pity me. I hate it. I wish they'd just ignore me.
I have a fear of the dark so I sleep with my mom. I'm eleven. She says I'm too old. But I can't. When I'm alone, I see things in the shadows.
I feel like I'm not important to anyone. I'm just here. Taking up space. No one would notice if I was gone. Not really.
I have a hard time asking for help because I think I should know better. I'm eleven. I should be able to do my own homework. But I can't.
I think I'm getting fat even though I haven't gained weight. I look in the mirror and see fat. My mom says I'm thin. She's lying.
I feel like I'm going to fail at everything I try. So why try? I don't join clubs. I don't try out for teams. I'll just fail anyway.
I have a ritual of spinning around before I sit down. I have to spin three times. Clockwise. If I don't, my chair will fall over. It happened once.
I think about hurting myself when I'm angry. I don't, but I think about it. I imagine cutting my arm. It makes the anger go away in my head.
I feel like I'm on a roller coaster of emotions all day. Happy, sad, angry, scared. All in one hour. I can't keep up. I'm exhausted.
I haven't seen my dad in three months. He said he'd come back for my birthday. He didn't. I don't think he loves me anymore.
I think I'm a mistake. My parents didn't plan me. They told me I was a surprise. That means mistake. I wasn't supposed to be born.
I have a fear of going to the doctor. I cry before we go. I shake in the waiting room. The needles, the cold stethoscope. I hate it.
I feel like I'm carrying my mom's sadness. It's heavy. She cries at night. I hear her. I want to help but I don't know how. So I just carry it.
I have a hard time making friends because I think they'll reject me. So I reject them first. I don't talk to anyone. Then I'm alone. But it's safer.
I think I'm ugly because kids at school said so. They called me names. I look in the mirror and I see what they see. Ugly.
I feel like I'm a bad person because I had a mean thought. I thought "I hate you" about my mom. I didn't mean it. But I thought it. That makes me bad.
I have to wash my hands until they feel "clean enough." It takes five minutes. Sometimes ten. My hands are red and raw. But I can't stop.
I think about running in front of a car sometimes. I wouldn't, but I think it. The idea is there. It scares me that I think it.
I feel like I'm not in control of my anger. It takes over. I break things. I scream. Then I calm down and I don't remember what happened. It's like a blackout.
I have a nightmare about my house burning down. I wake up smelling smoke. But there's no smoke. It's so real. I check the stove every night.
I think I'm the reason my grandma got sick. I visited her and then she got pneumonia. Maybe I brought germs. Maybe it's my fault.
I feel like I'm going to be sick all the time. The doctor said it's anxiety. But anxiety doesn't feel like this. This feels like dying.
I haven't talked to anyone at lunch in a month. I sit at the end of the table. I eat my sandwich. No one talks to me. I don't talk to them.
I think my best friend is mad at me because she didn't text back. It's been three hours. She always texts back. I did something wrong. I know it.
I have a fear of being alone. I follow my mom around the house. She goes to the bathroom, I stand outside the door. She says I'm clingy.
I feel like I'm not smart enough to be in this grade. I should be held back. Everyone is smarter than me. I don't belong here.
I have a hard time breathing when I think about my future. What if I never get a job? What if I'm homeless? I'm only nine but I worry.
I think about the time I embarrassed myself every single night. I said something dumb in class two years ago. I still cringe. I can't forget.
I feel like I'm a fraud. Everyone thinks I'm happy but I'm not. I'm good at pretending. But one day they'll find out. And they'll be disappointed.
I have a ritual of touching my nose before I go through a doorway. Any doorway. I tap my nose twice. I don't know why. I just have to.
I think my dad is going to leave because I'm bad. He already left once. He came back. But I think I'll make him leave again. I'm too much.
I feel like I'm made of glass. I'll break if someone touches me. I'm fragile. I can't handle anything. Even a loud noise makes me shatter.
I have a hard time sleeping because I'm scared of nightmares. I stay up as long as I can. I watch videos until my eyes close. Then the nightmares come anyway.
I think I hear whispers when I'm alone. They say my name. They say "come here." I look around and no one is there. It happens in my room.
I haven't eaten vegetables in a month because I'm scared of choking. I choked on a carrot once. Now I only eat soft foods. My mom is worried.
I feel like I'm not good at anything. I tried soccer, I was bad. I tried piano, I was bad. I tried drawing, I was bad. I give up.
I have a fear of the ocean. Even pictures make me nervous. The vastness. The darkness. The things under the water. No thank you.
I think my mom is sad because of me. She used to laugh. Now she's quiet. She looks tired. It's my fault. I make her sad.
I have a hard time concentrating on reading. The words move. They wiggle. I read the same sentence five times. I still don't know what it says.
I feel like I'm going to die young. I don't know why. I just have a feeling. Like my time is short. I'm scared to plan for the future.
I have a ritual of saying "goodnight" to every stuffed animal before I sleep. I have 14. I say goodnight to each one. If I miss one, I can't sleep.
I think about being in the hospital. It feels safe there. The nurses were nice. I didn't have to go to school. I wish I could go back.
I feel like I'm a burden to my teacher. I ask too many questions. I need extra help. She has other students. I'm taking her time.
I have a hard time being happy for my friends. I get jealous. When they win an award, I feel sick. I want to be happy for them but I can't.
I think I'm a disappointment to my parents. They wanted a doctor or a lawyer. I want to draw. They don't say it but I know they're disappointed.
I feel like I'm in a fog. Nothing is clear. Colors are dull. Sounds are muffled. I'm walking through a cloud. I can't find my way out.
I have a fear of being touched on my neck. I flinch. I pulled away from my aunt when she hugged me. She looked hurt. I couldn't explain.
I think about the time my parents yelled at me every day. The words they said. "You're so lazy." "Why can't you be like your sister?" I hear them in my head.
I feel like I'm not worthy of love. I push people away. I'm mean to my friends so they leave. Then I'm alone. That's what I deserve.
I have a hard time trusting anyone. Everyone leaves eventually. My dad left. My best friend moved. Why bother trusting? They'll just go.
I think I'm going crazy because I talk to myself. I have conversations in my head. I answer myself. Sometimes I say things out loud. People stare.
I feel like I'm falling and no one can catch me. I'm falling down a hole. I see the light get smaller. I keep falling. No one hears me scream.
I have a ritual of blinking twice before I speak. Blink blink, then talk. If I don't, I stutter. I used to stutter a lot. Now I blink instead.
I think about running away to a place where no one knows me. I could start over. New name, new life. No one would know how weird I am.
I feel like I'm a shadow of who I used to be. I used to be funny and loud. Now I'm quiet and sad. I don't even recognize myself.
I have a hard time going to birthday parties because of the noise. The screaming, the music, the balloons popping. I hide in the bathroom.
I think I'm not pretty enough. I look at other girls and they're so pretty. I'm plain. I have acne. I hate my nose. No one will ever like me.
I feel like I'm screaming inside but no one hears. I'm yelling for help but no sound comes out. I'm so loud in my head. But outside, I'm silent.
I have a fear of flying in airplanes. I've never been on one. But I've seen videos. The turbulence. The crashes. I'd rather drive.
I think about the time my friend died. It was last year. She was nine. Cancer. I went to the funeral. I still think about her every day.
I feel like I'm not important to anyone. I'm just background noise. In my family, in my class, in the world. I don't matter.
I have a hard time getting out of bed. It feels too hard. My body is heavy. My brain says "stay here." So I stay. I miss the bus a lot.
I think I'm a bad friend because I forgot her birthday. I was so busy with my own problems. I didn't even call her. She was sad. I'm terrible.
I feel like I'm drowning in homework. Page after page. I can't keep up. I stay up late. I'm so tired. My eyes burn. I want to give up.
I have a ritual of stepping on cracks. I try not to. I step over them. But sometimes I accidentally step and I have to step on another one to cancel it out.
I think about hurting myself to punish myself. When I do something bad, I want to feel pain. I deserve it. I pinch myself hard.
I feel like I'm not normal. Everyone else seems to know how to be a kid. I don't. I feel like an alien. Like I was dropped here by mistake.
I have a hard time saying no to people. I always say yes. Even when I don't want to. I'm scared they'll hate me. So I do things I don't want to do.
I think my parents are going to get divorced. They fight every day. They don't kiss anymore. They don't sit together. I hear my mom crying at night.
I feel like I'm carrying the weight of the world. On my shoulders. I'm so small. But the weight is so heavy. I can barely stand.
I have a fear of being laughed at. I don't tell jokes. I don't raise my hand. I don't wear bright colors. I try to be invisible so no one laughs.
I think about the future and it scares me. What if I'm homeless? What if I fail? What if I'm alone? My chest gets tight just thinking about it.
I feel like I'm not enough. Never enough. No matter how hard I try, it's not enough for my parents. For my teachers. For myself.
I have a hard time eating in front of people. I take small bites. I chew quietly. I'm scared of making noise. Of looking gross. I eat alone when I can.
I think I'm a coward because I didn't stand up for my friend. The kids were teasing her. I just stood there. I didn't say anything. I hate myself for that.
I feel like I'm in a prison in my own mind. The walls are my thoughts. The bars are my fears. I can't get out. I'm trapped in here.
I have a ritual of knocking on wood for good luck. I knock three times. I knock on my desk. On the wall. On my head. It's a lot.
I think about the time I got lost in the store. I was four. I couldn't find my mom. I cried. I still have nightmares about it.
I feel like I'm always tired, even when I sleep a lot. I slept for twelve hours and I'm still exhausted. It's like I have no batteries.
I have a hard time making decisions because I'm afraid of being wrong. What if I choose the wrong answer? What if I choose the wrong lunch? I freeze.
I think I'm a loser because I don't have many friends. I have one friend. Maybe she doesn't even like me. I'm such a loser.
I feel like I'm going to cry right now. My eyes are burning. My throat is tight. I don't even know why. I just feel so sad.
I have a fear of needles. I panic. I scream. I run. My mom has to hold me down. It's so embarrassing. I'm not a baby but I can't help it.
I think about my grandmother who passed away every night. I talk to her in my head. I ask her if she's okay. I miss her so much.
I feel like I'm not smart enough for my school. The other kids are so smart. They use big words. I don't understand. I feel stupid.
I have a hard time being around loud noises. I cover my ears. Fireworks, vacuum cleaners, even the blender. It hurts my ears and my brain.
I think I'm a bad kid because I broke a window. It was an accident. But I didn't tell my mom. I hid it. Now I feel so guilty. I'm a bad kid.
I feel like I'm invisible at school. The teachers don't call on me. The kids don't talk to me. I could be a ghost. No one would notice.
I have a ritual of checking my phone for messages every minute. I'm waiting for someone to text me. No one does. But I keep checking.
I think about being in the hospital like when I had surgery. I was asleep. I didn't feel anything. That was nice. No pain. No thoughts.
I feel like I'm going to have a heart attack. I'm nine. My chest hurts. My arm tingles. The doctor says it's anxiety. But what if he's wrong?
I have a hard time sleeping because I think about all my mistakes. I replay them. The time I tripped. The time I said the wrong thing. I can't stop.
I think I'm a failure because I failed a test. I studied so hard. But I still failed. I'm never going to succeed at anything.
I feel like I'm not loved. My parents say they love me. But they don't act like it. They don't hug me. They don't ask about my day. I don't feel loved.
I have a fear of throwing up in front of people. I feel nauseous a lot. I hold it in. I'm so scared I'll be sick in class. Everyone would see.
I think about running away from my problems. If I run far enough, maybe they can't find me. Maybe I can start over. Be a new person.
I feel like I'm a robot with no feelings. I don't get sad. I don't get happy. I just exist. I go through the motions. But I'm not alive.
I have a hard time talking to adults. My voice gets small. I mumble. They ask me to speak up. I try but I can't. I'm so scared.
I think I'm ugly because of my teeth. They're crooked. I don't smile in pictures. I cover my mouth when I laugh. I want braces so bad.
I feel like I'm going to pass out in gym class. My heart races. I see spots. The coach says I'm out of shape. But I think something is wrong.
I have a ritual of wearing my lucky socks on test days. They're blue with dinosaurs. I've worn them for every test this year. I'm scared not to.
I think about the time I fell and broke my arm. I replay it. The crack. The pain. The ambulance. I see it like a movie in my head.
I feel like I'm a burden to my friends. They always have to cheer me up. They always have to listen to my problems. They'll get tired of me.
I have a hard time being happy because I think something bad will happen. Every time I'm happy, I wait for the bad thing. It always comes.
I think I'm not good at anything. I tried to draw, to sing, to run. I'm bad at all of it. I have no talent. I'm useless.
I feel like I'm drowning in sadness. It's up to my neck. Soon it will cover my head. I'm gasping for air. I can't keep swimming.
I have a fear of the dentist. I cry in the chair. I shake. The drill sound makes me want to run. My mom has to hold my hand.
I think about my parents' voices yelling. I hear them when I'm alone. Even when they're not there. It's like a recording in my head.
I feel like I'm not worth the trouble. Why would anyone go out of their way for me? I'm not special. I'm not worth it.
I have a hard time focusing in class. My mind drifts. I think about my fish. I think about my lunch. I think about nothing. I miss everything the teacher says.
I think I'm a monster because I had a bad dream. I dreamed I hurt someone. It was so real. I woke up crying. Am I a monster?
I feel like I'm falling apart piece by piece. First my sleep. Then my appetite. Then my grades. I'm losing pieces of myself. Soon there will be nothing left.
I have a ritual of tapping my pencil before I write. Tap tap. Then I can write. If I don't tap, my hand won't move. I don't know why.
I think about the time I said something mean. I regret it. I apologized. But I still think about it. I still feel guilty. I can't forgive myself.
I feel like I'm not as good as other kids. They're faster, smarter, funnier. I'm just average. No, I'm below average. I'm not good enough.
I have a hard time being around my dad after he yelled. He yelled at me last week. Now I flinch when he walks by. I hide in my room.
I think I'm a disappointment. My mom wanted a daughter who does ballet. I do karate. She smiles but I know she's disappointed. I see it.
I feel like I'm going to be alone my whole life. No one will ever love me. I'll die alone. I'm ten but I know this.
I have a fear of the dark even with a nightlight. I need three nightlights. And the hall light. And my door open. I'm eleven. It's embarrassing.
I think about hurting myself when I feel numb. The pain wakes me up. It makes me feel something. Anything. I don't want to be numb.
I feel like I'm not real. Like I'm in a simulation. Nothing matters. I could do anything and it wouldn't matter. Is this real life?
I have a hard time asking for what I need. I need help. I need a hug. I need someone to listen. But I can't say it. The words won't come out.
I think I'm not lovable. No one has ever said "I love you" and meant it. Not really. They say it because they have to. I'm not lovable.
I feel like I'm carrying a secret that will destroy my family. Something happened to me. I can't tell. If I tell, everything will fall apart.
I have a ritual of praying before I go to sleep. I pray for safety. For my mom. For my dad. For my dog. If I miss a prayer, something bad will happen.
I think about the time I was bullied. It was last year. They called me names. They pushed me. I still hear their voices. I still feel scared.
I feel like I'm going to explode. My anger is a balloon. It's getting bigger and bigger. Soon it will pop. I'm scared of what I'll do.
I have a hard time eating in the cafeteria. Too many people. Too much noise. I take my tray to the library. The librarian lets me eat there.
I think I'm a freak because I have different interests. I like bugs. I like rocks. Other kids think I'm weird. Maybe I am weird.
I feel like I'm not seen. I can wave my arms and no one looks. I can scream and no one hears. I'm invisible. I don't exist.
I have a fear of being left behind. In the store, at the park, at school. What if they forget me? What if they leave and don't come back?
I think about running away to the woods. I'd build a shelter. I'd eat berries. I'd never come back. No one would find me.
I feel like I'm not good enough for my team. I'm the worst player. Everyone knows. The coach puts me on the bench. I don't blame him.
I have a hard time sleeping without my stuffed animal. I'm eleven. I still sleep with a bear. His name is Mr. Bear. I can't sleep without him.
I think I'm a bad person because I lied. I lied to my mom about where I was. She believed me. Now I feel sick with guilt. I'm so bad.
I feel like I'm going to be sick every morning. I run to the bathroom. Nothing comes out. But the feeling is there. Like I'm going to throw up.
I have a ritual of washing my face three times. Once with soap, once with water, once with soap again. My skin is dry. But I have to.
I think about the time I saw a car accident. I was in the backseat. The crash was loud. I saw the other car spin. I still see it.
I feel like I'm a ghost at my own birthday party. Everyone was there. But I wasn't really there. I was watching from outside. I felt nothing.
I have a hard time being happy for others. When my friend got a new bike, I felt jealous. I didn't say congratulations. I just walked away.
I think I'm not as strong as my sibling. My brother is tough. He doesn't cry. I cry all the time. I'm weak. He's strong. I'm not good enough.
I feel like I'm going to cry and never stop. Once I start, the tears keep coming. I can't turn it off. I cry for hours.
I have a fear of heights. Even stairs make me nervous. I hold the railing. I don't look down. My heart pounds.
I think about hurting my pet when I'm angry. I would never do it. But the thought is there. I think about kicking my dog. I hate that I think that.
I feel like I'm not in control. My body does things without my permission. I throw things. I hit walls. I'm not driving the bus. Something else is.
I have a hard time remembering the good days. I only remember the bad. The times I cried. The times I was scared. The good days are gone.
I think I'm a mistake. My parents didn't want a third kid. They told me I was an accident. That means mistake. I wasn't supposed to be here.
I feel like I'm going to fail everything. Every test. Every class. Every friendship. I'm a failure waiting to happen.
I have a ritual of checking the stove before I leave. I check it three times. Even if I didn't use it. I'm scared the house will burn down.
I think about the time my mom was crying. She was on the phone. She didn't know I was there. I caused it. I heard her say my name.
I feel like I'm not enough for anyone. Not for my mom, my dad, my friends. I try so hard. But I'm never enough. Never.
I have a hard time breathing when I think about school. The hallways, the lockers, the teachers. My chest gets tight. I can't breathe.
I think I'm not normal because I have weird thoughts. I think about hurting people. I think about jumping off things. I'm not normal.
I feel like I'm going to be abandoned. Everyone leaves. My dad left. My best friend left. My dog died. Everyone leaves. It's only a matter of time.
I have a fear of spiders. I scream when I see one. I freeze. I can't move. My mom has to kill it. I'm so scared.
I think about the time I fell down the stairs. I replay it. The thump thump thump. The pain in my back. I'm scared of stairs now.
I feel like I'm not worthy of happiness. Whenever I'm happy, I feel guilty. Like I don't deserve it. So I stop being happy. I stay sad.
I have a hard time sleeping because of the thoughts. They're loud. They won't shut up. I toss and turn. The clock ticks. I just want to sleep.
I think I'm a burden to my teacher. She has so many students. I need extra help. I take her time. I'm a burden.
I feel like I'm going to have a panic attack at school. My heart starts racing. I sweat. I feel like I'm dying. I have to go to the nurse.
I have a ritual of saying the same phrase over and over. "It's okay, it's okay, it's okay." I whisper it when I'm nervous. My friend asked why. I said nothing.
I think about the time my dad didn't come to my game. I looked for him in the stands. He wasn't there. I scored a goal and he missed it. I still think about that.
I feel like I'm not important. I'm just a kid. My opinions don't matter. My feelings don't matter. I don't matter.
I have a hard time being alone. I need someone near me. In the room, at least. If I'm alone, I panic. I call my mom. She comes.
I think I'm a bad influence on my little brother. He copies me. I hit things. He hits things. I'm teaching him to be bad. I'm a bad big sister.
I feel like I'm going to be sick from worry. My stomach churns. I have diarrhea. I can't eat. The worry is making me sick.
I have a fear of failing. It stops me from trying. I don't try out for teams. I don't enter contests. What if I fail? I'd rather not try.
I think about the time I was embarrassed in front of the whole class. I gave the wrong answer. Everyone laughed. I wanted to die. I still cringe.
I feel like I'm not safe even at home. Home is supposed to be safe. But my dad yells. My mom cries. I hide in my room. I'm not safe.
I have a hard time making eye contact. I look at the floor. I look at the wall. My teacher says to look at her. I try but I can't.
I think I'm not pretty like the other girls. They have nice hair and nice skin. I have pimples and frizzy hair. No one will ever like me.
I feel like I'm going to lose my mind. I'm not crazy yet. But I feel it coming. Like a storm. I'm scared of what I'll become.
I have a ritual of counting my steps. I have to end on an even number. If I end on odd, I walk in place until it's even. People stare.
I think about the time my pet died. I held her. She was warm. Then she wasn't. I felt her go. I still feel it in my hands.
I feel like I'm not good enough for my parents' love. They love me because they have to. Not because I deserve it. I don't deserve it.
I have a hard time sleeping because of the nightmares. I wake up screaming. My mom comes in. I don't tell her what I dreamed. It's too scary.
I think I'm a bad friend. I forgot to call my friend when her dog died. I was busy with my own stuff. I'm a terrible person.
I feel like I'm going to throw up from nervousness. Before a test, before a game, before anything. I keep a bag in my backpack.
I have a fear of being in trouble. I panic when someone says my name. My heart stops. What did I do wrong? I'm always scared I'm in trouble.
I think about the time I got lost. I was five. I couldn't find my mom. A stranger helped me. I still have nightmares about it.
I feel like I'm not seen by anyone. I can wave. I can jump. No one looks. I'm not seen. I don't exist.
I have a hard time eating when I'm stressed. I forget to eat. I go all day without food. Then I'm dizzy. But I'm not hungry.
I think I'm a failure at everything. I fail at school. I fail at friendships. I fail at being a daughter. I'm just a failure.
I feel like I'm going to be alone forever. No one will ever love me. I'll have no friends. I'll die alone. I know it.
I have a ritual of tapping my foot. I can't stop. Even when people tell me to stop. My leg shakes. I tap tap tap. It's not under my control.
I think about the time my parents forgot my birthday. They didn't plan a party. No cake. They said sorry. But I still think about it.
I feel like I'm not smart. I don't understand things. I read slowly. I mix up letters. The other kids are smart. I'm dumb.
I have a hard time talking about my feelings. I shut down. My mouth closes. My throat closes. I can't say anything. I just cry.
I think I'm a monster. I had a dream that I killed someone. It felt real. When I woke up, I checked my hands for blood. I'm a monster.
I feel like I'm going to die young. I don't know why. I just have a feeling. I don't plan for the future because I won't be there.
I have a fear of being judged. I don't wear what I want. I don't say what I think. I try to be normal. I'm scared of what people think.
I think about the time I got in trouble for something I didn't do. I was blamed. I was punished. I still feel angry. I didn't do it.
I feel like I'm not good at anything. I'm not athletic. I'm not artistic. I'm not smart. I'm just nothing. I have no skills.
I have a hard time trusting myself. I make bad decisions. I always choose wrong. I don't trust myself anymore. I ask other people what to do.
I think I'm a bad kid. I don't listen. I talk back. I break rules. I'm not a good kid. I'm bad. My parents say it.
I feel like I'm going to cry every day. The tears are always there. Behind my eyes. I hold them in. But they're so heavy.
I have a ritual of checking my windows are locked. I check before bed. I check in the middle of the night. I check in the morning. I'm scared of robbers.
I think about the time my friend moved away. I still miss her. I think about her every day. I wonder if she thinks about me.
I feel like I'm not important. Not to anyone. I'm a side character in everyone else's story. No one's main character.
I have a hard time being happy for others' success. When my friend won an award, I felt jealous. I didn't clap. I'm a bad person.
I think I'm not worthy. Not of love. Not of kindness. Not of good things. I don't deserve them. I deserve bad things.
I feel like I'm going to have a heart attack from stress. I'm so stressed. My chest hurts. My arm hurts. I'm nine. This can't be normal.
I have a fear of being late. I leave early for everything. I wake up early. I'm always the first one. I'm scared of being late.
I think about the time I failed a test. I cried. I tore it up. I didn't show my parents. I hid it. I still have the pieces.
I feel like I'm not loved by my dad. He doesn't say it. He doesn't hug me. He doesn't come to my things. I don't think he loves me.
I have a hard time sleeping because of the what-ifs. What if I fail? What if something happens to my mom? What if there's a fire? I can't sleep.
I think I'm a disappointment. To my parents. To my teachers. To myself. I disappoint everyone. I can't do anything right.
I feel like I'm going to explode with sadness. It's in my chest. It's so big. It's going to burst. And everyone will see how sad I am.
I have a ritual of saying sorry for everything. Sorry for bumping into you. Sorry for asking a question. Sorry for existing. I say sorry all day.
I think about the time I got yelled at in front of everyone. In class. The teacher screamed at me. Everyone stared. I wanted to disappear.
I feel like I'm not enough for my friends. They have other friends. Better friends. I'm just a backup. They'll leave me when someone better comes.
I have a hard time being around my mom when she's sad. I absorb her sadness. It becomes my sadness. I try to cheer her up. I fail.
I think I'm a burden. To everyone. My family, my teachers, my friends. They'd be happier without me. I take up too much space.
I feel like I'm going to be sick from all the worry. I worry about everything. School, friends, family, the future. My stomach hurts all the time.
I have a fear of being forgotten. When I die, no one will remember me. I'll be a name on a stone. That's it. I'll be forgotten.
I think about the time I hurt my brother's feelings. I called him stupid. He cried. I said sorry. But he still remembers. I still feel guilty.
I feel like I'm not normal. I'm weird. I don't fit in. I try to be normal but it doesn't work. I'm just weird.
I have a hard time asking for help because I'm ashamed. I should be able to do this myself. But I can't. I'm so ashamed.
I think I'm a failure as a daughter. I don't make my parents proud. I cause them stress. I'm not the daughter they wanted.
I feel like I'm going to lose everyone I love. My mom will die. My dad will leave. My friends will move. Everyone will go. I'll be alone.
I have a ritual of checking my backpack ten times. Pencil case? Check. Lunch? Check. Homework? Check. I do it over and over.
I think about the time I saw something scary online. It won't leave my head. A video of someone hurting themselves. I can't unsee it.
I feel like I'm not good at being a kid. Other kids have fun. They play. They laugh. I don't know how. I'm not good at being a kid.
I have a hard time being happy. It feels fake. When I smile, it's like I'm wearing a mask. I don't feel happy inside. I feel empty.
I think I'm a bad person. I have bad thoughts. I think about stealing. I think about lying. I think about hurting. I'm a bad person.
I feel like I'm going to have a panic attack. My heart is racing right now. I'm sweating. I feel like I'm dying. Help.
I have a fear of being alone in my room. I think someone is in the closet. Or under the bed. I leave the door open. I need the hall light on.
I think about the time my mom was in the hospital. I was so scared. I thought she was going to die. She didn't. But I still think about it.
I feel like I'm not worth saving. If I was drowning, would anyone jump in? No. I'm not worth saving. I'm not worth the trouble.
I have a hard time sleeping without a light on. I have a nightlight. And a lamp. And the hall light. I need light. The dark is scary.
I think I'm a mistake. I wasn't planned. I wasn't wanted. I'm a mistake. That's why I feel this way. I shouldn't exist.
I feel like I'm going to be forgotten. When I die, no one will come to my funeral. No one will remember my name. I'll be gone and no one will care.
I have a ritual of knocking on wood. I do it all day. Knock knock knock. I knock on my desk, on the wall, on my leg. My teacher told me to stop.
I think about the time I was teased for my weight. They called me fat. I still hear it. I look in the mirror and see fat. I hate my body.
I feel like I'm not good enough for my family. They're all successful. I'm not. I'm the black sheep. The failure. The disappointment.
I have a hard time eating in front of others. I hide. I eat in my room. I don't want anyone to see me. See the way I chew. See how much I eat.
I think I'm a failure. I failed at everything I've ever tried. I failed at school. I failed at making friends. I'm a failure.
I feel like I'm going to be alone. No one understands me. No one gets it. I'm alone in a crowd. I'm alone at home. I'm always alone.
I have a fear of throwing up. I avoid eating new things. I only eat safe foods. I'm scared I'll eat something bad and throw up in public.
I think about the time I fell and everyone laughed. I tripped on the stage. At the school assembly. Everyone laughed. I wanted to die.
I feel like I'm not seen. I'm a background character. I'm wallpaper. No one notices me. No one would miss me if I was gone.
I have a hard time trusting anyone. Everyone leaves. Everyone lies. I don't trust anyone anymore. Not even myself.
I think I'm not lovable. There's something wrong with me. Something unlovable. That's why no one stays. That's why I'm alone.
I feel like I'm going to cry forever. The tears won't stop. They keep coming. I've been crying for an hour. I can't stop.
I have a ritual of touching my necklace before I speak. I have a silver necklace. I touch it. Then I can talk. If I don't, my voice doesn't work.
I think about the time my dad left for work and never came back that day. He came back the next day. But I thought he was gone forever. I still think about it.
I feel like I'm not enough. I try so hard. I try to be good. I try to be smart. I try to be happy. But I'm never enough. Never.
I have a hard time being around loud people. They're too much. Their voices are too loud. Their energy is too high. I want to hide.
I think I'm a burden to the world. I use resources. I take up space. I cause problems. The world would be better off without me.
I feel like I'm going to disappear. Not die. Just fade away. Become less and less until I'm nothing. Like I was never here.
I have a fear of being embarrassed. I don't raise my hand. I don't tell jokes. I don't wear bright clothes. I try to be invisible so I can't be embarrassed.
I think about the time I said something stupid. I cringe. I replay it. I was trying to be funny and I said something dumb. Everyone got quiet. I still want to disappear.
I feel like I'm not important to anyone. I'm not a priority. I'm an afterthought. No one thinks about me. No one cares.
I have a hard time sleeping because of the scary thoughts. I think about death. I think about monsters. I think about my family dying. I can't sleep.
I think I'm a bad person because of my thoughts. My thoughts are dark. They're mean. They're scary. I don't act on them. But I think them. That makes me bad.
I feel like I'm going to throw up from anxiety. I'm nauseous right now. My mouth is watering. I think I'm going to be sick. I'm so anxious.
I have a ritual of counting ceiling tiles. I count them every time I'm in a room. I need to know the number. If I don't, I feel wrong.
I think about the time I was left out of a party. Everyone in my class was invited except me. I saw the pictures online. I cried for days.
I feel like I'm not good enough. No matter what I do. I got an A? Not good enough. I helped my mom? Not good enough. I'm never good enough.
I have a hard time being happy. It's like there's a wall. A big brick wall between me and happiness. I can't get over it. I can't get around it.
I think I'm a monster inside. On the outside, I look normal. But inside, I'm a monster. Dark. Twisted. Evil. I hide it.
I feel like I'm going to be sick all the time. The nausea never goes away. I live on crackers. I'm scared to eat anything else.
I have a fear of being touched unexpectedly. If someone taps my shoulder, I scream. I can't help it. My body reacts before my brain.
I think about the time I was blamed for something I didn't do. My teacher blamed me for stealing. I didn't steal. No one believed me. I still feel angry.
I feel like I'm not worth loving. I push people away. I'm mean to people who try to love me. Because I know I don't deserve it. I'm not worth loving.
I have a hard time sleeping. My brain won't shut up. It's like a radio playing static. Loud static. I can't turn it off. I just want to sleep.
I think I'm a failure as a person. Not just as a kid. As a human. I fail at being human. I don't know how to be normal. I'm a failure.
I feel like I'm going to fall apart. My pieces are loose. My seams are tearing. I'm going to fall apart and no one will be able to put me back together.
I have a ritual of stepping over cracks. Sidewalk cracks. Floor cracks. Any crack. I have to step over. If I step on a crack, I have to step on another one to fix it.
I think about the time I let my friend down. She needed me. I wasn't there. I chose to do something else. I let her down. I still feel guilty.
I feel like I'm not enough for myself. I can't even make myself happy. I can't comfort myself. I need someone else to do it. I'm not enough for me.
I don't want to feel this way anymore. I need help but I don't know how to ask. My mouth won't open. The words won't come. I'm stuck. Please help me.
My best friend hasn't eaten lunch all week. She says she's not hungry, but she looks really thin. I'm worried about her.
My little brother keeps pulling out his hair. There's a bald spot on his head now. He says it feels good. I don't know what to do.
There's a girl in my class who cries every day at recess. She hides behind the slide. I tried to talk to her but she ran away.
My friend told me she wants to die. She said it really quiet. Then she laughed and said just kidding. But I don't think she was kidding.
My cousin cuts his arms with a razor. He showed me. He made me promise not to tell. But I'm scared for him.
My classmate washes his hands like twenty times a day. His hands are all red and cracked. The teacher noticed but he said he just likes being clean.
My older sister stays in her room all the time now. She doesn't eat with us. She doesn't talk. She just stares at the wall. My mom is worried.
My friend said she hears voices. Like someone whispering her name. No one else hears it. She's scared to go to sleep.
My brother hits himself when he's angry. He punches his leg. He has bruises. He told me to shut up when I asked.
There's a kid at school who never talks. Like never. He just nods or shakes his head. The teacher thinks he's shy but I think something else is wrong.
My friend made herself throw up after lunch yesterday. I saw her in the bathroom. She said she ate too much. But she barely ate anything.
My little sister has nightmares every night. She wakes up screaming. My parents don't know what to do. She's only seven.
My classmate says he's not real. Like he's a robot or something. He said he feels like he's watching himself from outside his body. That sounds scary.
My friend's dad yells at her a lot. She has bruises on her arm. She says she fell. But I don't believe her.
My brother counts everything. Steps, tiles, cracks in the sidewalk. If he doesn't count, he says something bad will happen. He takes forever to walk anywhere.
There's a boy in my grade who never eats lunch. He just sits there. He's really skinny. I offered him my apple and he said no thanks.
My best friend told me she wants to run away. She packed a bag and hid it under her bed. She said she can't take it anymore. I don't know what "it" is.
My cousin says he sees shadows that aren't there. People in the corner of his eye. But when he turns, nothing's there. He's really scared.
My friend wears long sleeves even when it's super hot outside. I saw a cut on her wrist once when her sleeve slid up. She pulled it down fast.
My little brother has a ritual. He has to tap the door three times before he goes through. If he forgets, he goes back and does it. My mom thinks it's a phase.
There's a girl in my class who shakes all the time. Her hands shake when she writes. She says she's just cold but it's not cold.
My friend said she thinks about dying a lot. Not like she wants to do it. But her brain just goes there. She said it's exhausting.
My older brother gets so angry he breaks things. He punched a hole in the wall last week. Then he cried. He said he can't control it.
My classmate has a spot on her arm that she picks until it bleeds. She's been doing it for months. The nurse put a bandage on it but she took it off.
My friend stopped coming to school. It's been two weeks. She says her stomach hurts every morning. I think she's really sad.
My little sister is scared of everything. The dark, dogs, the vacuum, loud noises. She cries for hours. My mom is exhausted.
There's a kid at school who says he has a friend in his head. The friend tells him to do stuff. Sometimes bad stuff. I don't know if he's joking.
My cousin told me he doesn't want to be alive anymore. He said it like it was nothing. Like he was talking about the weather. I'm really scared for him.
My friend can't sleep. Like at all. She stays up until 3am and then she's exhausted at school. Her mom took her to the doctor but nothing helped.
My brother has to check the locks on the doors ten times before bed. He gets up in the middle of the night to check again. My parents are worried.
There's a girl in my class who never raises her hand anymore. She used to be so smart. Now she just stares at her desk. The teacher doesn't seem to notice.
My friend says she feels empty inside. Like there's nothing there. She said she'd rather feel sad than feel nothing. That sounds really hard.
My little brother is scared to eat. He choked on a grape once and now he's scared of food. He only drinks smoothies. My mom is really worried.
My classmate has panic attacks in class. His heart races and he can't breathe. He goes to the nurse almost every day. The other kids stare.
My friend lied to the school counselor. She said she was fine. But she's not fine. I know she's not. She just doesn't want anyone to think she's crazy.
My older sister stopped showering. It's been like four days. She wears the same hoodie every day. She used to care so much about how she looked.
There's a boy in my grade who never talks to anyone. He sits alone at lunch. He reads the same book over and over. I think he's really lonely.
My cousin is scared of germs. He uses hand sanitizer like fifty times a day. He won't touch door handles. He wears gloves sometimes.
My friend said she wants to disappear. Not die. Just vanish. Like she never existed. She said no one would even notice.
My little sister pulls her eyelashes out. She has a bald spot on her eyelid. She says she can't stop. It feels good to pull.
There's a girl in my class who throws up after lunch sometimes. I've seen her go to the bathroom and come back looking pale. I think she's making herself sick.
My friend told me her dad hits her. She said it quietly. Then she said don't tell anyone. I don't know what to do.
My brother has nightmares about the same thing every night. A dark figure standing over his bed. He's scared to go to sleep. He stays up watching videos.
My classmate says he can't focus. His brain is too loud. He got in trouble for not paying attention but I don't think it's his fault.
My friend is so tired all the time. She sleeps twelve hours and still feels exhausted. She said it's like walking through mud.
My little brother has a rash on his arm from scratching. He scratches when he's nervous. He doesn't even realize he's doing it.
There's a kid at school who says he's not real. Like he's in a dream. He pinches himself to see if he wakes up. He told me that and I got chills.
My cousin stopped playing his favorite video game. He used to play every day. Now he just lies on his bed. He says nothing is fun anymore.
My friend is scared of going outside. The world is too loud and bright. She stays in her room with the curtains closed. Her mom doesn't know what to do.
My older sister has a secret. She won't tell me what it is. But she cries at night. I hear her through the wall. I think something bad happened to her.
My classmate told me he wants to hurt himself. Not like kill himself. Just hurt. He said it makes him feel better. I didn't know what to say.
My friend thinks her teacher hates her. She said the teacher looks at her funny and calls on her to embarrass her. I don't see it but she's convinced.
My little brother can't swallow pills. He gags every time. He's nine and still needs liquid medicine. He feels like a baby.
There's a girl in my grade who has a friend in her head. The friend tells her she's stupid. She said it's not her own voice. That sounds really scary.
My cousin hasn't laughed in months. Like a real laugh. He smiles but it's fake. I can tell. I miss his real laugh.
My friend is scared of the dark. She's eleven and sleeps with a nightlight and the hall light on. Her parents say she's too old but she can't help it.
My brother has to arrange his toys in a certain order before he sleeps. If they're out of order, he can't sleep. He'll get up and fix them.
My classmate feels like she's choking even when she's not eating. She said there's a lump in her throat. The doctor said it's anxiety.
My friend forgot everything. She couldn't remember what she ate for breakfast. She couldn't remember what the teacher said five minutes ago. She's scared.
My little sister is scared to go to the bathroom alone. She makes my mom stand outside the door. She's ten. She thinks someone is in there.
There's a boy at school who shakes when he has to talk in class. His voice wobbles. His hands shake. Some kids laugh. I feel so bad for him.
My cousin says he feels like he's carrying a heavy backpack even when he's not. His shoulders hurt. The doctor said nothing is wrong.
My friend looked up her symptoms online. She thinks she has cancer. Her stomach hurts and she's tired. I told her to tell her mom but she's too scared.
My older brother won't be alone. He follows my mom around the house. He's twelve. She gets annoyed but he says he feels like he'll die if he's alone.
My classmate can't make decisions. Even what to eat for lunch is too hard. He just stands there. He asked me to choose for him.
My friend bit someone. She's eight. She bit a kid who took her toy. She said she just saw red and then she did it. She felt terrible after.
My little sister hears screaming at night. No one else does. She cries and says she's scared. My parents think she's imagining it.
There's a girl in my class who pretends to be happy. She laughs at jokes. She smiles. But I saw her crying in the bathroom. She's not okay.
My cousin has a stomachache every day before school. He goes to the nurse almost every morning. He's missed so much class.
My friend pulled out her hair. There's a bald patch near her ear. She said she does it when she's nervous. Her mom thinks she has alopecia.
My brother thinks he's not good enough. He says my parents deserve a better kid. He's so sad all the time. I don't know how to help him.
My classmate can't remember his math facts. He used to know them. Now his brain is empty. He failed a test and cried.
My friend sees spots when she stands up too fast. She likes it. She said it's like a break from everything. That worried me.
My little brother thinks someone broke into his room. His things were moved. My mom said no, but he's convinced. He sleeps with a bat now.
There's a boy at school who hasn't laughed in a month. Not a real laugh. I told a joke and everyone laughed except him. He just looked at me.
My cousin is scared to tell his mom he needs help. He thinks she'll think he's weak. He's only nine. He shouldn't have to be strong alone.
My friend checks under his bed five times before sleep. He uses a flashlight. He knows nothing is there but he still checks.
My older sister feels like she's in a bubble. She can see us but she can't reach us. She's alone even when we're in the same room.
My classmate ate a whole bag of candy and then made himself throw up. He told me. He said he felt guilty. I don't think that's normal.
My friend thinks his brain is broken. He can't focus. He can't remember. Other kids can do it. Why can't he?
My little brother doesn't like to be touched on his back. He flinches. He won't say why. I think something happened to him.
There's a girl in my grade who has chest pain. The doctor said it's anxiety. But she says it feels like a heart attack. She's scared she'll die.
My cousin fainted in class once. The room went spinny and he fell. The nurse came. He was so embarrassed. Now he's scared it'll happen again.
My friend doesn't want to do anything. Not watch TV. Not play games. He just lies in bed. His mom says he's lazy. I don't think that's it.
My brother has bad thoughts. He thinks about hurting people. He would never do it. But the thoughts scare him. He thinks he's a monster.
My classmate has to touch every doorknob twice. If he doesn't, he thinks his family will get sick. He told me that. It takes him forever to leave a room.
My friend wakes up at 3am every night. He can't fall back asleep. He just lies there and thinks. He's so tired all the time.
My little sister feels like she doesn't belong in our family. She thinks she was adopted. She's not. But she really believes it.
There's a boy at school who scratched his face when he was angry. He has a red mark. He told everyone his cat did it. He doesn't have a cat.
My cousin has the same nightmare about drowning. He wakes up gasping. He's scared to go to sleep now.
My friend won't eat in front of people. He eats in the bathroom at school. He takes his sandwich into a stall. He's so ashamed.
My older brother thinks his teacher is out to get him. He says she gives him bad grades on purpose. I think he's paranoid.
My classmate has trouble breathing when he thinks about his parents fighting. They yell a lot. He hides in his closet.
My friend wrote a suicide note. He showed me. Then he tore it up and threw it away. He said he doesn't want to die but he thinks about it.
My little brother says he's not real. Like a video game character. He says nothing matters. I'm really scared for him.
There's a girl in my class who wears the same shirt every day. She has five of the same shirt. She says she feels wrong if she wears anything else.
My cousin doesn't feel sad. He feels nothing. He says that's worse. He misses feeling things.
My friend thinks about hurting his little brother sometimes. He would never do it. But the thought scares him. He thinks something is wrong with him.
My older sister can't stop eating. She eats until her stomach hurts. She hides wrappers under her bed. I found them once.
My classmate thinks everyone is staring at him in the hallway. He walks with his head down. He says he hears them whispering.
My friend hasn't been to school in two weeks. He says he's sick. But I think he's scared. His mom doesn't know what to do.
My little brother has a lump in his throat that won't go away. He swallows and swallows. The doctor said it's stress.
There's a boy in my grade who thinks he's going to fail everything. He's so scared of the test tomorrow. He studied but he says he can't remember.
My cousin hid under his desk during a fire drill. He couldn't move. The teacher had to pull him out. Everyone saw. He was so embarrassed.
My friend picks his skin when he's nervous. He has little scabs on his arms. He peels them off. They bleed.
My little sister feels like she's not in control of her body. Sometimes her arm moves without her telling it. She hits herself. It's like someone else is driving.
There's a girl at school who's scared of elevators. She takes the stairs even if it's ten floors. She says the small box makes her sweat.
My brother thinks he has a secret illness. His stomach hurts. His head hurts. He's tired. But the doctors say he's fine. He doesn't believe them.
My friend doesn't want to grow up. He wants to stay a kid forever. Adults are sad and tired. He sees his parents. He doesn't want to be like them.
My classmate punched a wall and broke his hand. He said it felt good. The pain made the anger go away. He told his parents he fell.
My cousin feels like a burden to his family. He says they'd be better off without him. He's ten. That broke my heart.
My older brother has to repeat his sentences three times. He says them, then again, then again. It's annoying but he says he has to.
My friend thinks he's losing his mind. His thoughts are scrambled. He hears things. He sees things. He's really scared.
My little sister is scared to be happy. She says something bad always happens after. So she tries not to be happy.
There's a boy in my class who hasn't talked to his dad in a week. They live in the same house. They walk past each other like ghosts.
