My cousin thinks there's a monster under his bed. He's ten. He knows it's silly. But he still checks. Every night with a flashlight.
My friend feels like he's going to explode with anger. He runs outside and punches the air. His mom thinks he's having a tantrum.
My little brother hates his body. He looks in the mirror and feels sick. He's only eight. He shouldn't feel that way.
There's a girl in my grade who can't trust adults because one hurt her. She won't say who. But she flinches when teachers come near.
My friend wakes up from nightmares and can't go back to sleep. His heart pounds. He lies in the dark thinking about the dream.
My older sister thinks she's the reason our parents fight. They argue about her grades and her attitude. She thinks if she wasn't here, they'd be happy.
My classmate has to wash his clothes after one wear. Even if he only wore them for an hour. His mom is annoyed with all the laundry.
My cousin feels like he's in a tunnel. He can see the light but he can't reach it. He's stuck in the dark.
My friend hurt himself with a needle. Just a little prick. He said it made him feel calm. The pain was a relief.
My little brother can't remember anything. He wrote himself a note but forgot where he put it. His brain is like a sieve.
There's a boy at school who thinks his brother hates him. His brother never looks at him anymore. He used to play with him. Now he just stays in his room.
My cousin is scared of throwing up. He only eats safe foods – crackers, bread, applesauce. He weighs himself every day.
My friend feels like his head is full of cotton. Everything is muffled. He can't think clearly. It's like he's underwater.
My older sister thinks about running away every day. She has a map. She knows which bus to take. She has money saved. She just hasn't done it yet.
My classmate doesn't like to be praised. He said it makes him feel weird. When someone says good job, he wants to hide.
My little brother has to close his eyes and count to ten before he enters a room. Any room. He doesn't know why. He just has to.
There's a girl in my class who thinks she's a failure. She tried to draw a dog and it looked like a blob. She tore it up and cried.
My cousin hasn't smiled in a week. Not a real smile. His face looks frozen. His mom asked him to smile for a picture and it looked fake.
My friend feels invisible at home. He talks and his parents don't respond. He asks for help and they say in a minute but they never come.
My little sister has a hard time swallowing food. She chews forever. She's scared she'll choke. She drinks water after every bite.
There's a boy in my grade who thinks his dad is mad at him even when he's quiet. His dad's silence is loud. He tries to be smaller.
My cousin has a rash on his face from crying and wiping his tears. His eyes are puffy. His nose is red. He doesn't want to go to school.
My friend feels like he's not smart enough for his friends. They talk about things he doesn't understand. He thinks they'll leave him.
My older brother wants to scream but nothing comes out. He opens his mouth and no sound. Like he's mute.
My classmate has a ritual of tapping his foot three times before he answers a question. In class, before he raises his hand. Tap tap tap.
My friend thinks he's going to be alone forever. No one will marry him. No one will love him. He's ten and he's convinced.
My little brother has no energy to play with his friends. He just wants to lie down. They asked him to come over and he said no.
There's a girl at school who feels like her heart is going to beat out of her chest for no reason. She puts her hand on her chest. It's so fast.
My cousin doesn't want to take his medicine anymore. It makes him feel like a zombie. He pretends to swallow it and hides it under his tongue.
My friend saw a shadow person in his closet. It was tall with no face. He hid under his blankets and didn't sleep all night.
My little sister has a hard time looking people in the eye. She looks at the floor. She looks at their shoes. Her teacher said it's respectful to look at people but she can't.
There's a boy in my class who feels like he's carrying a secret so heavy he can't breathe. He wants to tell someone but he's scared.
My cousin hits himself in the leg when he's frustrated. He makes a fist and punches his thigh. He has bruises. His shorts cover them.
My friend thinks his mom is sick and not telling him. She sleeps a lot. She doesn't eat. He asked and she said she's fine but he doesn't believe her.
My older brother has nightmares about being chased every single night. A man in a mask. He wakes up sweaty. He's so tired.
My classmate feels like he doesn't deserve good things. When something good happens, he waits for the bad. He can't just be happy.
My little brother hasn't eaten a meal in two days. He's just not hungry. Food looks gross. My mom made his favorite dinner and he pushed it around.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks her friend is lying about liking her. She heard her whisper to someone else. She thinks she's being made fun of.
My cousin has a fear of the number 13. He avoids it. He won't sit in seat 13. He won't go to page 13. Bad things happen on 13.
My friend feels like he's going to cry all the time. Even when nothing is wrong. The tears are right there behind his eyes. He holds them in.
My little sister stopped doing her homework. She said what's the point? She's going to fail anyway. Her teacher called my mom.
There's a boy at school who thinks he's a bad kid. He doesn't listen and he talks back. His parents say he's disrespectful. He tries to be good but he can't.
My cousin has to line up his shoes perfectly or he can't leave the house. They have to be straight. Even. His mom yells that they're going to be late.
My friend feels trapped in his own head. Like he's in a prison. His thoughts are the walls. He can't get out.
My older brother doesn't like to be kissed by his grandma. He said it feels wrong. Her lips are wet. She holds his face. He freezes.
My classmate has a hard time falling asleep because his thoughts race. They go around and around. About school, friends, what he did wrong.
My little brother thinks about dying when he's sad. It makes him feel better. Like, if nothing works out, he has a way out. He doesn't tell anyone that.
There's a girl in my class who hasn't played with her toys in months. They just sit there. She used to love Legos. Now she doesn't care.
My cousin feels like a robot. He does what he's told but he doesn't feel anything. He eats when they say, sleeps when they say. He's not alive inside.
My friend has a fear of dogs because one bit him when he was little. Now he crosses the street when he sees a dog. Even a little one. His heart races.
My little sister thinks her teacher thinks she's annoying. She sighs when she asks a question. She rolls her eyes. Now she doesn't ask anything.
There's a boy in my grade who has to wear his socks inside out or he can't walk. The seam bothers him. His mom buys seamless socks but he still turns them inside out.
My cousin feels like he's going to throw up every morning before school. He sits by the toilet. Nothing comes out. Just dry heaving.
My friend has a habit of biting his lip until it bleeds. He does it when he's nervous. His lip is always cracked. It hurts but he can't stop.
My older brother thinks he's not as good as me. He says I'm perfect. I get awards, I have friends. He thinks he's the mistake. That's not true.
My classmate has a hard time being in crowds. He feels like he can't breathe. Everyone is too close. He starts to sweat. He has to leave.
My little brother feels like a ghost in our house. He walks around and no one sees him. He says something and no one responds. He's not really here.
There's a girl at school who wrote a list of all her flaws. She said it's very long. She has it hidden in her drawer. She adds to it when she thinks of something new.
My cousin has to check his backpack ten times before school. Did he bring his lunch? His homework? His pencil? He checks and checks.
My friend thinks about the day his dog died every single day. He saw her get hit by a car. He was holding her leash. He let go. He thinks it's his fault.
My little sister feels like she's drowning in her feelings. Sadness is water in her nose, in her mouth. She can't breathe.
There's a boy in my class who hasn't laughed at a joke in a long time. He smiles so people don't ask. But inside, nothing. Jokes are just words.
My cousin thinks people are nicer to him because they feel sorry for him. They know he's sad. They pity him. He hates it.
My friend has a fear of the dark so he sleeps with his mom. He's eleven. She says he's too old. But he can't help it. He sees things in the shadows.
My older brother feels like he's not important to anyone. He's just here. Taking up space. No one would notice if he was gone.
My classmate has a hard time asking for help because he thinks he should know better. He's eleven. He should be able to do his own homework. But he can't.
My little brother thinks he's getting fat even though he hasn't gained weight. He looks in the mirror and sees fat. My mom says he's thin. He thinks she's lying.
There's a girl in my grade who feels like she's going to fail at everything she tries. So she doesn't try. She doesn't join clubs. She doesn't try out for teams.
My cousin has a ritual of spinning around before he sits down. He has to spin three times clockwise. If he doesn't, his chair will fall over. It happened once.
My friend thinks about hurting himself when he's angry. He doesn't, but he thinks about it. He imagines cutting his arm. It makes the anger go away in his head.
My little sister feels like she's on a roller coaster of emotions all day. Happy, sad, angry, scared. All in one hour. She's exhausted.
There's a boy at school who hasn't seen his dad in three months. His dad said he'd come back for his birthday. He didn't. He doesn't think his dad loves him anymore.
My cousin thinks he's a mistake. His parents didn't plan him. They told him he was a surprise. He thinks that means mistake.
My friend has a fear of going to the doctor. He cries before they go. He shakes in the waiting room. The needles, the cold stethoscope. He hates it.
My older brother feels like he's carrying our mom's sadness. It's heavy. She cries at night. He hears her. He wants to help but he doesn't know how.
My classmate has a hard time making friends because he thinks they'll reject him. So he rejects them first. He doesn't talk to anyone. Then he's alone.
My little brother thinks he's ugly because kids at school said so. They called him names. He looks in the mirror and sees what they see. Ugly.
There's a girl in my class who feels like a bad person because she had a mean thought. She thought "I hate you" about her mom. She didn't mean it. But she thought it.
My cousin has to wash his hands until they feel "clean enough." It takes five minutes. Sometimes ten. His hands are red and raw. He can't stop.
My friend thinks about running in front of a car sometimes. He wouldn't, but he thinks it. The idea is there. It scares him that he thinks it.
My little sister feels like she's not in control of her anger. It takes over. She breaks things. She screams. Then she calms down and doesn't remember what happened.
There's a boy in my grade who has a nightmare about his house burning down. He wakes up smelling smoke. But there's no smoke. He checks the stove every night.
My cousin thinks he's the reason his grandma got sick. He visited her and then she got pneumonia. He thinks he brought germs. He thinks it's his fault.
My friend feels like he's going to be sick all the time. The doctor said it's anxiety. But he says anxiety doesn't feel like this. This feels like dying.
My older brother hasn't talked to anyone at lunch in a month. He sits at the end of the table. He eats his sandwich. No one talks to him. He doesn't talk to them.
My classmate thinks his best friend is mad at him because she didn't text back. It's been three hours. She always texts back. He thinks he did something wrong.
My little brother has a fear of being alone. He follows my mom around the house. She goes to the bathroom, he stands outside the door. She says he's clingy.
There's a girl at school who feels like she's not smart enough to be in her grade. She thinks she should be held back. Everyone is smarter than her.
My cousin has a hard time breathing when he thinks about his future. What if he never gets a job? What if he's homeless? He's only nine but he worries.
My friend thinks about the time he embarrassed himself every single night. He said something dumb in class two years ago. He still cringes. He can't forget.
My little sister feels like a fraud. Everyone thinks she's happy but she's not. She's good at pretending. But one day they'll find out.
There's a boy in my class who has a ritual of touching his nose before he goes through a doorway. Any doorway. He taps his nose twice. He doesn't know why.
My cousin thinks his dad is going to leave because he's bad. His dad already left once and came back. He thinks he'll make him leave again.
My friend feels like he's made of glass. He'll break if someone touches him. He's fragile. He can't handle anything. Even a loud noise makes him shatter.
My older brother has a hard time sleeping because he's scared of nightmares. He stays up as long as he can. He watches videos until his eyes close.
My classmate thinks he hears whispers when he's alone. They say his name. They say "come here." He looks around and no one is there.
My little brother hasn't eaten vegetables in a month because he's scared of choking. He choked on a carrot once. Now he only eats soft foods.
There's a girl in my grade who feels like she's not good at anything. She tried soccer, she was bad. She tried piano, she was bad. She gave up.
My cousin has a fear of the ocean. Even pictures make him nervous. The vastness, the darkness, the things under the water.
My friend thinks his mom is sad because of him. She used to laugh. Now she's quiet. She looks tired. He thinks he makes her sad.
My little sister has a hard time concentrating on reading. The words move. They wiggle. She reads the same sentence five times and still doesn't know what it says.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to die young. He doesn't know why. He just has a feeling. He's scared to plan for the future.
My cousin has a ritual of saying "goodnight" to every stuffed animal before he sleeps. He has 14. He says goodnight to each one. If he misses one, he can't sleep.
My friend thinks about being in the hospital. It felt safe there. The nurses were nice. He didn't have to go to school. He wishes he could go back.
My older brother feels like a burden to his teacher. He asks too many questions. He needs extra help. She has other students. He's taking her time.
My classmate has a hard time being happy for his friends. He gets jealous. When they win an award, he feels sick. He wants to be happy for them but he can't.
My little brother thinks he's a disappointment to our parents. They wanted a doctor or a lawyer. He wants to draw. He knows they're disappointed.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's in a fog. Nothing is clear. Colors are dull. Sounds are muffled. She can't find her way out.
My cousin has a fear of being touched on his neck. He flinches. He pulled away from his aunt when she hugged him. She looked hurt. He couldn't explain.
My friend thinks about the time his parents yelled at him every day. The words they said. "You're so lazy." "Why can't you be like your sister?" He hears them in his head.
My little sister feels like she's not worthy of love. She pushes people away. She's mean to her friends so they leave. Then she's alone.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time trusting anyone. Everyone leaves eventually. His dad left. His best friend moved. Why bother trusting?
My cousin thinks he's going crazy because he talks to himself. He has conversations in his head. He answers himself. Sometimes he says things out loud.
My friend feels like he's falling and no one can catch him. He's falling down a hole. He sees the light get smaller. No one hears him scream.
My older brother has a ritual of blinking twice before he speaks. Blink blink, then talk. If he doesn't, he stutters. He used to stutter a lot.
My classmate thinks about running away to a place where no one knows him. He could start over. New name, new life. No one would know how weird he is.
My little brother feels like a shadow of who he used to be. He used to be funny and loud. Now he's quiet and sad. He doesn't recognize himself.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time going to birthday parties because of the noise. The screaming, the music, the balloons popping. She hides in the bathroom.
My cousin thinks he's not pretty enough. He looks at other boys and they're so handsome. He has acne. He hates his nose. No one will ever like him.
My friend feels like he's screaming inside but no one hears. He's yelling for help but no sound comes out. He's so loud in his head. But outside, he's silent.
My little sister has a fear of flying in airplanes. She's never been on one. But she's seen videos. The turbulence, the crashes. She'd rather drive.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time his friend died. It was last year. She was nine. Cancer. He went to the funeral. He still thinks about her every day.
My cousin feels like he's not important to anyone. He's just background noise. In his family, in his class, in the world. He doesn't matter.
My friend has a hard time getting out of bed. It feels too hard. His body is heavy. His brain says "stay here." So he stays. He misses the bus a lot.
My older brother thinks he's a bad friend because he forgot her birthday. He was so busy with his own problems. He didn't even call her.
My classmate feels like he's drowning in homework. Page after page. He can't keep up. He stays up late. He's so tired. His eyes burn.
My little brother has a ritual of stepping on cracks. He tries not to. He steps over them. But sometimes he accidentally steps and has to step on another one to cancel it out.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about hurting herself to punish herself. When she does something bad, she wants to feel pain. She deserves it. She pinches herself hard.
My cousin feels like he's not normal. Everyone else seems to know how to be a kid. He doesn't. He feels like an alien. Like he was dropped here by mistake.
My friend has a hard time saying no to people. He always says yes. Even when he doesn't want to. He's scared they'll hate him.
My little sister thinks her parents are going to get divorced. They fight every day. They don't kiss anymore. She hears her mom crying at night.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's carrying the weight of the world on his shoulders. He's so small. But the weight is so heavy.
My cousin has a fear of being laughed at. He doesn't tell jokes. He doesn't raise his hand. He tries to be invisible so no one laughs.
My friend thinks about the future and it scares him. What if he's homeless? What if he fails? What if he's alone? His chest gets tight.
My older brother feels like he's never enough. No matter how hard he tries, it's not enough for his parents, his teachers, himself.
My classmate has a hard time eating in front of people. He takes small bites. He chews quietly. He's scared of making noise. He eats alone when he can.
My little brother thinks he's a coward because he didn't stand up for his friend. Kids were teasing her. He just stood there. He hates himself for that.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's in a prison in her own mind. The walls are her thoughts. The bars are her fears. She can't get out.
My cousin has a ritual of knocking on wood for good luck. He knocks three times. On his desk, on the wall, on his head. It's a lot.
My friend thinks about the time he got lost in the store. He was four. He couldn't find his mom. He cried. He still has nightmares about it.
My little sister feels like she's always tired, even when she sleeps a lot. She slept for twelve hours and she's still exhausted. Like she has no batteries.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time making decisions because he's afraid of being wrong. What if he chooses the wrong answer? He freezes.
My cousin thinks he's a loser because he doesn't have many friends. He has one friend. Maybe she doesn't even like him. He's such a loser.
My friend feels like he's going to cry right now. His eyes are burning. His throat is tight. He doesn't even know why.
My older brother has a fear of needles. He panics. He screams. He runs. His mom has to hold him down. He's so embarrassed.
My classmate thinks about his grandmother who passed away every night. He talks to her in his head. He asks if she's okay. He misses her so much.
My little brother feels like he's not smart enough for his school. The other kids are so smart. They use big words. He doesn't understand.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time being around loud noises. She covers her ears. Fireworks, vacuum cleaners, even the blender.
My cousin thinks he's a bad kid because he broke a window. It was an accident. But he didn't tell his mom. He hid it. Now he feels so guilty.
My friend feels invisible at school. The teachers don't call on him. The kids don't talk to him. He could be a ghost. No one would notice.
My little sister has a ritual of checking her phone for messages every minute. She's waiting for someone to text her. No one does. But she keeps checking.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about being in the hospital like when he had surgery. He was asleep. He didn't feel anything. That was nice.
My cousin feels like he's going to have a heart attack. He's nine. His chest hurts. His arm tingles. The doctor says it's anxiety but what if he's wrong?
My friend has a hard time sleeping because he thinks about all his mistakes. He replays them. The time he tripped. The time he said the wrong thing.
My older brother thinks he's a failure because he failed a test. He studied so hard. But he still failed. He's never going to succeed at anything.
My classmate feels like he's not loved. His parents say they love him. But they don't act like it. They don't hug him. He doesn't feel loved.
My little brother has a fear of throwing up in front of people. He feels nauseous a lot. He holds it in. He's so scared he'll be sick in class.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about running away from her problems. If she runs far enough, maybe they can't find her. Maybe she can start over.
My cousin feels like a robot with no feelings. He doesn't get sad or happy. He just exists. He goes through the motions. But he's not alive.
My friend has a hard time talking to adults. His voice gets small. He mumbles. They ask him to speak up. He tries but he can't.
My little sister thinks she's ugly because of her teeth. They're crooked. She doesn't smile in pictures. She covers her mouth when she laughs.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to pass out in gym class. His heart races. He sees spots. The coach says he's out of shape.
My cousin has a ritual of wearing his lucky socks on test days. They're blue with dinosaurs. He's worn them for every test this year.
My friend thinks about the time he fell and broke his arm. He replays it. The crack, the pain, the ambulance. He sees it like a movie.
My older brother feels like a burden to his friends. They always have to cheer him up. They always have to listen to his problems. They'll get tired of him.
My classmate has a hard time being happy because he thinks something bad will happen. Every time he's happy, he waits for the bad thing.
My little brother thinks he's not good at anything. He tried to draw, to sing, to run. He's bad at all of it. He has no talent.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's drowning in sadness. It's up to her neck. Soon it will cover her head. She's gasping for air.
My cousin has a fear of the dentist. He cries in the chair. He shakes. The drill sound makes him want to run. His mom has to hold his hand.
My friend thinks about his parents' voices yelling. He hears them when he's alone. Even when they're not there. It's like a recording in his head.
My little sister feels like she's not worth the trouble. Why would anyone go out of their way for her? She's not special.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time focusing in class. His mind drifts. He thinks about his fish, his lunch, nothing. He misses everything.
My cousin thinks he's a monster because he had a bad dream. He dreamed he hurt someone. It was so real. He woke up crying.
My friend feels like he's falling apart piece by piece. First his sleep, then his appetite, then his grades. Soon there will be nothing left.
My older brother has a ritual of tapping his pencil before he writes. Tap tap. Then he can write. If he doesn't tap, his hand won't move.
My classmate thinks about the time he said something mean. He regrets it. He apologized. But he still thinks about it. He can't forgive himself.
My little brother feels like he's not as good as other kids. They're faster, smarter, funnier. He's just average. No, below average.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time being around her dad after he yelled. He yelled at her last week. Now she flinches when he walks by.
My cousin thinks he's a disappointment. His mom wanted a daughter who does ballet. He does karate. She smiles but he knows she's disappointed.
My friend feels like he's going to be alone his whole life. No one will ever love him. He'll die alone. He's ten but he knows.
My little sister has a fear of the dark even with a nightlight. She needs three nightlights, the hall light, and her door open. She's eleven.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about hurting himself when he feels numb. The pain wakes him up. It makes him feel something.
My cousin feels like he's not real. Like he's in a simulation. Nothing matters. He could do anything and it wouldn't matter.
My friend has a hard time asking for what he needs. He needs help, a hug, someone to listen. But the words won't come out.
My older brother thinks he's not lovable. No one has ever said "I love you" and meant it. Not really. They say it because they have to.
My classmate feels like he's carrying a secret that will destroy his family. Something happened to him. He can't tell. If he tells, everything will fall apart.
My little brother has a ritual of praying before he goes to sleep. He prays for safety. For his mom, his dad, his dog. If he misses a prayer, something bad will happen.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about the time she was bullied. It was last year. They called her names, pushed her. She still hears their voices.
My cousin feels like he's going to explode. His anger is a balloon getting bigger and bigger. Soon it will pop. He's scared of what he'll do.
My friend has a hard time eating in the cafeteria. Too many people, too much noise. He takes his tray to the library. The librarian lets him eat there.
My little sister thinks she's a freak because she has different interests. She likes bugs and rocks. Other kids think she's weird. Maybe she is weird.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's not seen. He can wave his arms and no one looks. He can scream and no one hears. He's invisible.
My cousin has a fear of being left behind. In the store, at the park, at school. What if they forget him? What if they leave and don't come back?
My friend thinks about running away to the woods. He'd build a shelter. He'd eat berries. He'd never come back. No one would find him.
My older brother feels like he's not good enough for his team. He's the worst player. Everyone knows. The coach puts him on the bench.
My classmate has a hard time sleeping without his stuffed animal. He's eleven. He still sleeps with a bear named Mr. Bear. He can't sleep without him.
My little brother thinks he's a bad person because he lied. He lied to his mom about where he was. She believed him. Now he feels sick with guilt.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's going to be sick every morning. She runs to the bathroom. Nothing comes out. But the feeling is there.
My cousin has a ritual of washing his face three times. Once with soap, once with water, once with soap again. His skin is dry. But he has to.
My friend thinks about the time he saw a car accident. He was in the backseat. The crash was loud. He saw the other car spin. He still sees it.
My little sister feels like a ghost at her own birthday party. Everyone was there. But she wasn't really there. She was watching from outside.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time being happy for others. When his friend got a new bike, he felt jealous. He didn't say congratulations.
My cousin thinks he's not as strong as his sibling. His brother is tough. He doesn't cry. He cries all the time. He's weak.
My friend feels like he's going to cry and never stop. Once he starts, the tears keep coming. He can't turn it off. He cries for hours.
My older brother has a fear of heights. Even stairs make him nervous. He holds the railing. He doesn't look down. His heart pounds.
My classmate thinks about hurting his pet when he's angry. He would never do it. But the thought is there. He hates that he thinks that.
My little brother feels like he's not in control. His body does things without his permission. He throws things, hits walls. He's not driving the bus.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time remembering the good days. She only remembers the bad. The times she cried, the times she was scared.
My cousin thinks he's a mistake. His parents didn't want a third kid. They told him he was an accident. That means mistake.
My friend feels like he's going to fail everything. Every test, every class, every friendship. He's a failure waiting to happen.
My little sister has a ritual of checking the stove before she leaves. She checks it three times. Even if she didn't use it. She's scared the house will burn down.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time his mom was crying. She was on the phone. She didn't know he was there. He heard her say his name.
My cousin feels like he's not enough for anyone. Not for his mom, his dad, his friends. He tries so hard. But he's never enough.
My friend has a hard time breathing when he thinks about school. The hallways, the lockers, the teachers. His chest gets tight.
My older brother thinks he's not normal because he has weird thoughts. He thinks about hurting people, jumping off things. He's not normal.
My classmate feels like he's going to be abandoned. Everyone leaves. His dad left, his best friend left, his dog died. It's only a matter of time.
My little brother has a fear of spiders. He screams when he sees one. He freezes, can't move. His mom has to kill it. He's so scared.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about the time she fell down the stairs. She replays it. The thump thump thump. The pain. She's scared of stairs now.
My cousin feels like he's not worthy of happiness. Whenever he's happy, he feels guilty. Like he doesn't deserve it. So he stops being happy.
My friend has a hard time sleeping because of the thoughts. They're loud. They won't shut up. He tosses and turns. He just wants to sleep.
My little sister thinks she's a burden to her teacher. Her teacher has so many students. She needs extra help. She takes her time. She's a burden.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to have a panic attack in class. His heart starts racing, he sweats. He has to go to the nurse.
My cousin has a ritual of saying the same phrase over and over. "It's okay, it's okay, it's okay." He whispers it when he's nervous.
My friend thinks about the time his dad didn't come to his game. He looked for him in the stands. He wasn't there. He scored a goal and his dad missed it.
My older brother feels like he's not important. He's just a kid. His opinions don't matter. His feelings don't matter. He doesn't matter.
My classmate has a hard time being alone. He needs someone near him. In the room, at least. If he's alone, he panics. He calls his mom.
My little brother thinks he's a bad influence on his younger sibling. He copies him. He hits things. He's teaching him to be bad.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's going to be sick from worry. Her stomach churns. She has diarrhea. She can't eat.
My cousin has a fear of failing. It stops him from trying. He doesn't try out for teams or enter contests. What if he fails?
My friend thinks about the time he was embarrassed in front of the whole class. He gave the wrong answer. Everyone laughed. He wanted to die.
My little sister feels like she's not safe even at home. Home is supposed to be safe. But her dad yells, her mom cries. She hides in her room.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time making eye contact. He looks at the floor, the wall. His teacher says to look at her. He tries but he can't.
My cousin thinks he's not pretty like the other girls. They have nice hair and nice skin. He has pimples and frizzy hair. No one will ever like him.
My friend feels like he's going to lose his mind. He's not crazy yet. But he feels it coming, like a storm. He's scared of what he'll become.
My older brother has a ritual of counting his steps. He has to end on an even number. If he ends on odd, he walks in place until it's even.
My classmate thinks about the time his pet died. He held her. She was warm, then she wasn't. He felt her go. He still feels it in his hands.
My little brother feels like he's not good enough for his parents' love. They love him because they have to. Not because he deserves it.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time sleeping because of nightmares. She wakes up screaming. Her mom comes in. She doesn't tell her what she dreamed.
My cousin thinks he's a bad friend. He forgot to call his friend when her dog died. He was busy with his own stuff. He's a terrible person.
My friend feels like he's going to throw up from nervousness. Before a test, before a game, before anything. He keeps a bag in his backpack.
My little sister has a fear of being in trouble. She panics when someone says her name. Her heart stops. What did she do wrong?
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time he got lost. He was five. He couldn't find his mom. A stranger helped him. He still has nightmares.
My cousin feels like he's not seen by anyone. He can wave, jump. No one looks. He's not seen. He doesn't exist.
My friend has a hard time eating when he's stressed. He forgets to eat. He goes all day without food. Then he's dizzy.
My older brother thinks he's a failure at everything. He fails at school, friendships, being a son. He's just a failure.
My classmate feels like he's going to be alone forever. No one will ever love him. He'll have no friends. He'll die alone.
My little brother has a ritual of tapping his foot. He can't stop. Even when people tell him to stop. His leg shakes. Tap tap tap.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about the time her parents forgot her birthday. No party, no cake. They said sorry. But she still thinks about it.
My cousin feels like he's not smart. He doesn't understand things. He reads slowly, mixes up letters. Other kids are smart. He's dumb.
My friend has a hard time talking about his feelings. He shuts down. His mouth closes, his throat closes. He can't say anything. He just cries.
My little sister thinks she's a monster. She had a dream that she killed someone. It felt real. When she woke up, she checked her hands for blood.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to die young. He doesn't know why. He doesn't plan for the future because he won't be there.
My cousin has a fear of being judged. He doesn't wear what he wants, say what he thinks. He tries to be normal. He's scared of what people think.
My friend thinks about the time he got in trouble for something he didn't do. He was blamed and punished. He still feels angry. He didn't do it.
My older brother feels like he's not good at anything. He's not athletic, artistic, or smart. He's just nothing. He has no skills.
My classmate has a hard time trusting himself. He makes bad decisions, always chooses wrong. He doesn't trust himself anymore.
My little brother thinks he's a bad kid. He doesn't listen, talks back, breaks rules. He's not a good kid. He's bad. His parents say it.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's going to cry every day. The tears are always there behind her eyes. She holds them in.
My cousin has a ritual of checking his windows are locked. Before bed, in the middle of the night, in the morning. He's scared of robbers.
My friend thinks about the time his friend moved away. He still misses her. He thinks about her every day. He wonders if she thinks about him.
My little sister feels like she's not important. Not to anyone. She's a side character in everyone else's story. No one's main character.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time being happy for others' success. When his friend won an award, he felt jealous. He didn't clap.
My cousin thinks he's not worthy. Not of love, kindness, or good things. He doesn't deserve them. He deserves bad things.
My friend feels like he's going to have a heart attack from stress. He's so stressed. His chest hurts, his arm hurts. He's nine. This can't be normal.
My older brother has a fear of being late. He leaves early for everything. He wakes up early. He's always the first one.
My classmate thinks about the time he failed a test. He cried and tore it up. He didn't show his parents. He hid it. He still has the pieces.
My little brother feels like he's not loved by his dad. His dad doesn't say it, doesn't hug him, doesn't come to his things. He doesn't think his dad loves him.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time sleeping because of the what-ifs. What if she fails? What if something happens to her mom? What if there's a fire?
My cousin thinks he's a disappointment. To his parents, teachers, himself. He disappoints everyone. He can't do anything right.
My friend feels like he's going to explode with sadness. It's in his chest, so big. It's going to burst. Everyone will see how sad he is.
My little sister has a ritual of saying sorry for everything. Sorry for bumping into you, sorry for asking a question, sorry for existing.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time he got yelled at in front of everyone. The teacher screamed at him. Everyone stared. He wanted to disappear.
My cousin feels like he's not enough for his friends. They have other friends, better friends. He's just a backup. They'll leave him.
My friend has a hard time being around his mom when she's sad. He absorbs her sadness. It becomes his sadness. He tries to cheer her up. He fails.
My older brother thinks he's a burden. To his family, teachers, friends. They'd be happier without him. He takes up too much space.
My classmate feels like he's going to be sick from all the worry. He worries about everything. School, friends, family, the future. His stomach hurts all the time.
My little brother has a fear of being forgotten. When he dies, no one will remember him. He'll be a name on a stone. That's it.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about the time she hurt her brother's feelings. She called him stupid. He cried. She said sorry. But she still feels guilty.
My cousin feels like he's not normal. He's weird, doesn't fit in. He tries to be normal but it doesn't work. He's just weird.
My friend has a hard time asking for help because he's ashamed. He should be able to do this himself. But he can't. He's so ashamed.
My little sister thinks she's a failure as a daughter. She doesn't make her parents proud. She causes them stress. She's not the daughter they wanted.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to lose everyone he loves. His mom will die, his dad will leave, his friends will move. He'll be alone.
My cousin has a ritual of checking his backpack ten times. Pencil case? Check. Lunch? Check. Homework? Check. Over and over.
My friend thinks about the time he saw something scary online. It won't leave his head. A video of someone hurting themselves. He can't unsee it.
My older brother feels like he's not good at being a kid. Other kids have fun, play, laugh. He doesn't know how. He's not good at being a kid.
My classmate has a hard time being happy. It feels fake. When he smiles, it's like a mask. He doesn't feel happy inside. He feels empty.
My little brother thinks he's a bad person. He has bad thoughts about stealing, lying, hurting. He's a bad person.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's going to have a panic attack. Her heart is racing, she's sweating. She feels like she's dying.
My cousin has a fear of being alone in his room. He thinks someone is in the closet or under the bed. He leaves the door open and the hall light on.
My friend thinks about the time his mom was in the hospital. He was so scared. He thought she was going to die. She didn't. But he still thinks about it.
My little sister feels like she's not worth saving. If she was drowning, would anyone jump in? No. She's not worth the trouble.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time sleeping without a light on. He has a nightlight, a lamp, and the hall light. He needs light. The dark is scary.
My cousin thinks he's a mistake. He wasn't planned, wasn't wanted. He's a mistake. That's why he feels this way. He shouldn't exist.
My friend feels like he's going to be forgotten. When he dies, no one will come to his funeral. No one will remember his name.
My older brother has a ritual of knocking on wood. He does it all day. Knock knock knock. On his desk, the wall, his leg. His teacher told him to stop.
My classmate thinks about the time he was teased for his weight. They called him fat. He still hears it. He looks in the mirror and sees fat.
My little brother feels like he's not good enough for his family. They're all successful. He's not. He's the black sheep, the failure.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time eating in front of others. She hides, eats in her room. She doesn't want anyone to see her.
My cousin thinks he's a failure. He failed at everything he's ever tried. School, making friends. He's a failure.
My friend feels like he's going to be alone. No one understands him. No one gets it. He's alone in a crowd, alone at home. Always alone.
My little sister has a fear of throwing up. She avoids eating new things, only eats safe foods. She's scared she'll eat something bad and throw up in public.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time he fell and everyone laughed. He tripped on the stage at the school assembly. Everyone laughed. He wanted to die.
My cousin feels like he's not seen. He's a background character, wallpaper. No one notices him. No one would miss him if he was gone.
My friend has a hard time trusting anyone. Everyone leaves, everyone lies. He doesn't trust anyone anymore. Not even himself.
My older brother thinks he's not lovable. There's something wrong with him, something unlovable. That's why no one stays.
My classmate feels like he's going to cry forever. The tears won't stop. He's been crying for an hour. He can't stop.
My little brother has a ritual of touching his necklace before he speaks. He has a silver necklace. He touches it, then he can talk. If he doesn't, his voice doesn't work.
There's a girl in my grade who thinks about the time her dad left for work and never came back that day. He came back the next day. But she thought he was gone forever.
My cousin feels like he's not enough. He tries so hard to be good, smart, happy. But he's never enough. Never.
My friend has a hard time being around loud people. Their voices are too loud, their energy too high. He wants to hide.
My little sister thinks she's a burden to the world. She uses resources, takes up space, causes problems. The world would be better off without her.
There's a boy at school who feels like he's going to disappear. Not die, just fade away. Become less and less until he's nothing.
My cousin has a fear of being embarrassed. He doesn't raise his hand, tell jokes, or wear bright clothes. He tries to be invisible.
My friend thinks about the time he said something stupid. He cringes and replays it. He was trying to be funny and said something dumb. Everyone got quiet.
My older brother feels like he's not important to anyone. He's not a priority, an afterthought. No one thinks about him. No one cares.
My classmate has a hard time sleeping because of scary thoughts. Death, monsters, his family dying. He can't sleep.
My little brother thinks he's a bad person because of his thoughts. His thoughts are dark, mean, scary. He doesn't act on them, but he thinks them.
There's a girl in my class who feels like she's going to throw up from anxiety. She's nauseous right now, mouth watering. She's so anxious.
My cousin has a ritual of counting ceiling tiles. He counts them every time he's in a room. He needs to know the number. If he doesn't, he feels wrong.
My friend thinks about the time he was left out of a party. Everyone in his class was invited except him. He saw pictures online. He cried for days.
My little sister feels like she's not good enough. No matter what she does. An A? Not good enough. Helping mom? Not good enough. Never good enough.
There's a boy in my grade who has a hard time being happy. It's like a brick wall between him and happiness. He can't get over or around it.
My cousin thinks he's a monster inside. On the outside he looks normal, but inside he's dark, twisted, evil. He hides it.
My friend feels like he's going to be sick all the time. The nausea never goes away. He lives on crackers, scared to eat anything else.
My older brother has a fear of being touched unexpectedly. If someone taps his shoulder, he screams. His body reacts before his brain.
My classmate thinks about the time he was blamed for something he didn't do. His teacher blamed him for stealing. No one believed him. He still feels angry.
My little brother feels like he's not worth loving. He pushes people away, is mean to people who try to love him. Because he knows he doesn't deserve it.
There's a girl at school who has a hard time sleeping. Her brain won't shut up, like a radio playing static. She can't turn it off.
My cousin thinks he's a failure as a person. Not just as a kid, as a human. He fails at being human. He doesn't know how to be normal.
My friend feels like he's going to fall apart. His pieces are loose, his seams tearing. He's going to fall apart and no one will be able to put him back together.
My little sister has a ritual of stepping over cracks. Sidewalk cracks, floor cracks. She has to step over. If she steps on a crack, she has to step on another to fix it.
There's a boy in my class who thinks about the time he let his friend down. She needed him and he wasn't there. He chose to do something else. He still feels guilty.
My cousin feels like he's not enough for himself. He can't even make himself happy. He can't comfort himself. He needs someone else to do it.
My friend has a hard time asking for help. His mouth won't open, the words won't come. He's stuck. He needs help but doesn't know how to ask.
My older brother told me his friend stopped coming to school. He said his friend's mom is sick and his friend is really sad. He doesn't know how to help.
My classmate said his little sister won't eat. She's seven and she's scared of getting fat. She only eats lettuce. That's not normal, right?
My cousin's best friend cuts himself. He showed my cousin the marks on his thighs. My cousin is really scared and doesn't know if he should tell an adult.
There's a girl in my grade who told me her mom cries every night. She thinks her mom is depressed. She doesn't know what to do.
My friend's little brother has to wash his hands for five minutes every time he touches anything. His hands are bleeding. His parents don't know.
My little sister's classmate hasn't spoken in two weeks. Not at school. She just writes notes. The teacher is worried.
There's a boy on my bus who told me he wishes he was never born. He said it like it was nothing. I didn't know what to say.
My cousin's older sister stopped eating with the family. She only eats alone in her room. She's lost a lot of weight. My cousin is worried.
My friend's dad yells at him all the time. My friend said he's scared to go home. He asked if he could stay at my house.
My classmate's little brother has nightmares every night about a monster. He's six. He screams so loud the neighbors heard. My classmate is exhausted.
There's a girl at school who told me she counts everything. Steps, tiles, even her own breaths. She can't stop. She said it's exhausting.
My cousin's friend said he sees a man in the corner of his room. No one else sees him. He's terrified to sleep. His parents think he's lying.
My friend's older brother punched a hole in the wall yesterday. Then he cried for an hour. My friend doesn't know what to do.
My little brother has a kid in his class who never plays at recess. He just sits on the bench. He doesn't talk to anyone. My brother feels bad for him.
There's a boy in my grade who told me he made himself throw up after lunch. He said he ate too much. But he only had a sandwich.
My cousin's little sister is scared of everything. She won't go to school, won't go to the park, won't even go to the backyard. She stays inside all day.
My friend's mom doesn't get out of bed. She stays in her room with the curtains closed. My friend makes his own meals. He's nine.
My classmate told me he thinks about running away. He has a bag packed under his bed. He said he almost left last night.
There's a girl in my grade who flinches when the teacher raises her hand. She covers her face. I think someone hits her at home.
My cousin's friend hasn't showered in a week. He wears the same hoodie every day. He used to be so clean. Something changed.
My friend's little brother pulls his hair out when he's nervous. He has bald spots. He's seven. My friend doesn't know how to help him.
There's a boy at school who told me he's not real. He said life is a dream. He pinched me to prove it. It hurt. He said "see, still a dream."
My cousin's older brother hasn't left his room in three days. He just lies in bed. My aunt is really worried. She doesn't know what to do.
My friend's sister has a rash on her arms from scratching. She scratches when she's nervous. She doesn't even notice anymore.
My classmate's dad comes home drunk. He yells and throws things. My classmate hides in the closet with his little brother. He told me quietly.
There's a girl at school who told me she wants to disappear. Not die. Just disappear. So no one would miss her. That's even sadder.
My cousin's friend has a ritual. He has to touch his nose, then his ear, then his nose again before every test. He forgot once and failed. Now he's convinced.
My friend's little sister is scared to eat. She choked on a grape last month and now she only drinks smoothies. She's losing weight.
There's a boy in my grade who has panic attacks in the hallway. He hyperventilates and falls to his knees. The teachers have to carry him to the nurse.
My cousin told me his mom is sad all the time. She doesn't laugh anymore. She just stares at the TV. He's scared she'll never be happy again.
My friend's older brother is really mean to himself. He calls himself stupid and ugly. He punches his own leg. My friend heard him through the wall.
My classmate's little brother has to check under his bed twenty times before sleep. He counts. 1,2,3... up to 20. If he misses one, he starts over.
There's a girl in my grade who said she feels like a robot. She goes to school, comes home, does homework, sleeps. No feelings. Just tasks.
My cousin's friend is scared of throwing up. He hasn't eaten a vegetable in months. He only eats white foods. Bread, rice, pasta. His mom is worried.
My friend's dad never talks. He just sits in his chair. My friend tries to tell him about his day and his dad doesn't respond. He feels invisible.
There's a boy at school who told me he has a plan. He didn't say what plan. But his face was serious. I'm scared for him.
My cousin's little sister has a fear of the dark so intense she sleeps in her parents' bed. She's nine. They can't get her to leave.
My friend's mom takes a lot of pills. My friend counted. He's scared she's going to take too many. He hides some of them.
My classmate's brother gets so angry he breaks his own toys. Then he cries and tries to glue them back together. It's a cycle.
There's a girl in my grade who told me she's not eating lunch anymore. She said she's training for something. But she's already so thin.
My cousin's friend hears a voice that tells him he's worthless. He says the voice sounds like his dad. But his dad doesn't live with him anymore.
My friend's little brother is scared to go to the bathroom by himself. He's ten. He makes my friend stand outside the door. My friend is tired of it.
There's a boy at school who stopped playing video games. He used to talk about them all the time. Now he just says he doesn't care anymore.
My cousin's older sister has a rash on her neck from scratching. She scratches when she thinks about school. She's missing a lot of school.
My friend's dad doesn't come to his baseball games anymore. He used to come to every one. Now he says he's busy. My friend thinks he doesn't love him.
My classmate's little brother has to say "goodnight" to every stuffed animal. He has 20. It takes him 15 minutes. My classmate thinks it's OCD.
There's a girl in my grade who told me she's not sleeping. She stays up all night watching videos. She's scared of the nightmares.
My cousin's friend told me he wants to hurt himself. He said he deserves it. He said he's a bad person. I told him he's not. He didn't believe me.
My friend's older sister is really quiet now. She used to be loud and funny. Now she just nods or shrugs. My friend misses her old self.
There's a boy at school who told me his stomach hurts every day. The nurse knows his name. He's been to the doctor but they can't find anything. I think it's anxiety.
My cousin said his best friend hasn't smiled in weeks. Not a real smile. He fakes it but my cousin can tell. He's really worried about him. He asked me what to do. I didn't know.
